attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

file.content:
# Notes

This directory contains various notes and documentation related to the project.
Each file is organized by topic for easy reference.

By default there are chores-*.md and todo.md. Chores are general notes
about tasks and todo.md contains short term tasks and their status.

In the future we I expect we may want to create a "notes"
database to better manage the information, TBD.

Examples chore file:
```
# Chores-01.md
 
General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
how and why of a particular chore.

## Create a binary that lists jj info 

This binary should list the changeID, commitID, and description title
and using `jj-lib`
```

## Workflow and conventions

Bot-facing workflow, versioning, and code conventions live in
[`../CLAUDE.md`](../CLAUDE.md). Start there for:

- **Versioning during development** — single-step vs multi-step,
  `-N` pre-release suffixes, done-marker discipline.
- **Code Conventions** — doc comments on every file / fn / method,
  `// OK: …` justifications on `unwrap*` calls, ask-on-ambiguity,
  stuck detection.
- **Commit-Push-Finalize Flow** — two-checkpoint per-step
  discipline with hard stop after finalize.

## Todo format

Todo.md contains two main sections "Todo" and "Done" each item is a
short explanations of a tasks and links to more details using 1 or more
references.

Multiple references must be separated: `[2],[3]` not `[2,3]` or `[2][3]`.
In markdown, `[2,3]` is a single ref key (won't resolve) and `[2][3]`
is parsed as display text `2` with ref key `3` (so `[2]` won't resolve).

Examples:

# Todo
- Add new feature X [details](features.md#feature-x)
- Fix bug Y [1]

# Done
- Fixed issue Z [2],[3]

[1]: bugs.md#bug-y
[2]: issues.md#issue-z
[3]: fixes.md#fix-z

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress


## Todo

A markdown list of task to do in the near feature

 - Check for non-tracking-remote bookmarks during push and sync preflight as
   bookmarks that aren't trackng can't bre pushed.
 - Allow `vc-x1 push` to work on code or bot repo together or independantly and
   specifically we should be able to "squash" in th code repo just as we do in
   the bot repo.
 - Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
 - Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
 - Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
 - Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
 - Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
 - Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
 - Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
 - Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
 - Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]
- push polish: --dry-run, --step, non-tty detection, gitignore warning (0.37.0-5) [48]
- push docs + workflow migration — CLAUDE.md rewrite + README section (0.37.0) [48]

# References

[4]: /notes/chores-01.md#refactor-and-add-desc-subcommand
[5]: /notes/chores-01.md#claude-repo-issue-070-dev0-through-dev2

[37]: /notes/chores-03.md#windows-symlink-support

[49]: /notes/chores-05.md#claudemd-refresh--memory-migration-0361
[50]: /notes/chores-05.md#sync-improvements--single-repo-support--quieter-dry-run-0363

content.text:
Acquainted. Here's the lay of the land:

**Project**: `vc-x1` v0.37.0 — Rust CLI wrapping `jj-lib` for working with paired jj-git repos (app + `.claude` session).

**Structure** (from `CLAUDE.md`): dual-repo workflow, `vc-x1 push` is the canonical commit-push-finalize wrapper with two interactive approval gates (review, message). Hard stop after finalize. Doc comments required on every file/fn; `// OK: …` justifications required on `unwrap*` calls.

**Current state**:
- App repo (`.`): clean, on `main` at `cad1490` ("chores: A couple additional tasks in todo").
- `.claude` repo: live session activity (expected); on `main` at `203aff29`.
- Just finished the **0.37.0 push subcommand** arc (multi-step `-0` through `-5`, then the `0.37.0` done marker).

**Next up** (top of `notes/todo.md`):
1. Check for non-tracking-remote bookmarks during push/sync preflight.
2. Let `vc-x1 push` operate on code or bot repo independently, with `squash` on the code repo too.
3. Richer bookmark enumeration [52].

**`src/`** modules: `chid`, `clone`, `desc`, `fix_desc`, `finalize`, `init`, `list`, `push`, `show`, `symlink`, `sync`, `validate_desc`, plus helpers (`common`, `desc_helpers`, `logging`, `test_fixture`, `test_helpers`, `toml_simple`).

What would you like to tackle?

message.content:
We'd changed the todo and I personally wanted to commit it so I tried `vc-x1 push` and it worked kind-of.
It figured since I didn't supply a --tile or --body it went into "interactive" mode, good.
I entered the Title and then wrote and exited, `wq!`. I was then expectiung to be asked to enter the body but instead I was apparently supposed to enter both in the first vi session. I probably didn't read the instructions correctly. What was I supposed to do?

content.text:
Both go in **one editor session**. The template (`push-message.txt`) opens with this header:

```
# Write the commit title on the first line (target ~50 chars).
# Lines starting with `#` are ignored.
# Leave everything blank to abort the push.
#
# Body goes after a blank line. ochid trailers are appended
# per-repo automatically — don't add them here.
```

The expected shape is:

```
Your title here

Your body paragraph here.
- bullet
- bullet
```

`#` lines are stripped, then the content is split on the **first blank line** — everything before is the title, everything after is the body (`src/push.rs:962-983`). Save once with `:wq` and you're done; there is no second prompt.

So in your case, with only a title typed and no blank-line+body, `parse_message` would have set `title = <what you typed>` and `body = ""`. The push wouldn't have prompted again — it would have proceeded with an empty body to the message-approval gate.

If you'd like, the help text in `vc-x1 push --help` / the editor template could be made more explicit ("write title, blank line, then body — all in this one file"), since the current wording assumes you read it carefully. Want me to tighten the template wording?

content.content:
parses; `BANNER` emitted as `info!` at the start of every
  run except the detached `finalize --exec` re-entry, and
  suppressed when the active subcommand has `--no-label` set
  (so `chid -L` / `desc -L` / `list -L` / `show -L` stay
  script-parseable)

#### 0.37.0-5 — polish

- `src/push.rs` — `--dry-run`: skip every side-effect subprocess
  (preflight / commit-app / commit-claude / bookmark-both /
  push-app / finalize-claude), emit `[dry-run] would run: ...`
  lines instead; review still shows the diff (that's the *point*
  of dry-run); state file not persisted in dry-run so a later
  real run starts clean. `--step`: after each completed stage
  (with a next stage pending), prompt `[y/N]` to continue;
  `--yes` short-circuits; non-tty without `--yes` errors fast.
  Non-tty detection via `std::io::IsTerminal` — review stage
  errors when stdin isn't a tty and `--yes` isn't set; message
  stage does the same before launching `$EDITOR`.
  `check_gitignore_coherence` — reads `.gitignore` at the
  workspace root and warns (non-fatal) when the configured
  state-dir name isn't present. Helps catch the "user changed
  `[push].state-dir` in `.vc-config.toml` and forgot to update
  `.gitignore`" case.

#### 0.37.0 — docs + workflow migration (done marker)

- `src/push.rs` — fold `vc-x1 sync --no-dry-run` into `preflight`
  as the first step (divergence resolved before cargo burns
  cycles); matches the 0.36.3 design's "push preflight calls
  sync" note.
- `CLAUDE.md` (both repos, byte-identical) — rewrite
  Commit-Push-Finalize Flow around `vc-x1 push`: intro says
  "use push"; drops the two-checkpoint manual ceremony
  (Checkpoint 1 / Checkpoint 2 / Finalize the .claude repo);
  keeps "After finalize: stop and wait" and "Late changes
  after push"; adds a "Manual finalize fallback" subsection
  for `--no-finalize` and post-failure cases; pre-step sync
  section reframed as "still useful" (push runs sync internally
  but running it earlier surfaces divergence sooner).
- `README.md` — new `### push` subsection under Usage: stage
  table, flag table, state-file config, link to this chore's
  per-step record.
- `notes/chores-05.md` — per-step record filled in for this
  step; block now complete through `0.37.0`.

**Status: shipped.** The push subcommand design is implemented,
tested, and documented. The manual Commit-Push-Finalize Flow is
retired in CLAUDE.md — `vc-x1 push <bookmark>` is the primary
entry point going forward.

**Status: shipped.** The push subcommand design is implemented,
tested, and documented. The manual Commit-Push-Finalize Flow is
retired in CLAUDE.md — `vc-x1 push <bookmark>` is the primary
entry point going forward.

## Tighten `$EDITOR` template wording (0.37.1)

First dogfood of the interactive `vc-x1 push` flow exposed two
papercuts in the editor template:

1. The header was structured "title on first line / blank line /
   body after" — easy to read as "title now, body in a second
   prompt later". A user typed only the title, `:wq`'d, and was
   surprised when push proceeded with an empty body instead of
   re-prompting.
2. The template required a literal blank line between title and
   body. If a user typed title-then-body with no blank between,
   `parse_message`'s `splitn(2, "\n\n")` made the *whole thing*
   the title and the body empty — silent footgun.

The fix is to make the template's contract explicit and to relax
the parser so the blank-line separator is not user-required.

- `src/push.rs` — template header rewritten:

  > Leave as-is to abort. Enter the Title on the first line,
  > optionally followed by the Body on subsequent lines. The
  > blank line between Title and Body is inserted automatically,
  > and the ochid trailer is appended per-repo automatically —
  > don't add either here.
  > Lines starting with `#` are ignored.

  States the contract up front (one editor session, title on
  line 1, body optional after) and surfaces the two
  auto-insertions (separator blank line, ochid trailer) so the
  user knows not to type either.

- `src/push.rs` — `parse_message` rewritten: first non-comment
  line is the title, remainder (trimmed) is the body. Blank
  line between is allowed but not required. Doc comment updated.
  The "blank line between title and body in the final commit
  message" responsibility lives in the commit-stage `format!`
  calls (`"{title}\n\n{body}\n\nochid: ..."`), which were
  already there — so no change needed downstream of the parser.

- `src/push.rs` — `parse_message_cases` test grows two cases:
  title+body with no blank line (new behavior), and a body
  with internal blank lines preserved (regression guard for
  the multi-paragraph body case the old splitn-based code
  also handled correctly but is now exercised by a different
  code path).

- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — version `0.37.0` → `0.37.1`.

- `notes/todo.md` — Done entry added.

**Why single-step `0.37.1` rather than `0.37.0-6`:** the 0.37.0
arc was closed by the done marker; this is a focused mechanical
fix discovered during first-real-use, not a continuation of the
push design work. Keeps the dev ladder of a feature distinct
from post-ship polish.

fix discovered during first-real-use, not a continuation of the
push design work. Keeps the dev ladder of a feature distinct
from post-ship polish.

lines: tested, and documented. The manual Commit-Push-Finalize Flow is
retired in CLAUDE.md — `vc-x1 push <bookmark>` is the primary
+First dogfood of the interactive `vc-x1 push` flow exposed two
+1. The header was structured "title on first line / blank line /
+   body after" — easy to read as "title now, body in a second
+   prompt later". A user typed only the title, `:wq`'d, and was
+   surprised when push proceeded with an empty body instead of
+2. The template required a literal blank line between title and
+   body. If a user typed title-then-body with no blank between,
+   the title and the body empty — silent footgun.
+The fix is to make the template's contract explicit and to relax
+the parser so the blank-line separator is not user-required.
+  > Leave as-is to abort. Enter the Title on the first line,
+  > optionally followed by the Body on subsequent lines. The
+  > blank line between Title and Body is inserted automatically,
+  > and the ochid trailer is appended per-repo automatically —
+  States the contract up front (one editor session, title on
+  line 1, body optional after) and surfaces the two
+  auto-insertions (separator blank line, ochid trailer) so the
+  user knows not to type either.
+  line is the title, remainder (trimmed) is the body. Blank
+  line between is allowed but not required. Doc comment updated.
+  The "blank line between title and body in the final commit
+  message" responsibility lives in the commit-stage `format!`
+  already there — so no change needed downstream of the parser.
+  title+body with no blank line (new behavior), and a body
+  with internal blank lines preserved (regression guard for
+  the multi-paragraph body case the old splitn-based code
+  also handled correctly but is now exercised by a different
+arc was closed by the done marker; this is a focused mechanical
+fix discovered during first-real-use, not a continuation of the
+push design work. Keeps the dev ladder of a feature distinct

content.text:
All green. Here's the summary of what changed:

**Direct answer to your question:** the `$EDITOR` opens **one file** with both title and body in the same session. The old template said "Body goes after a blank line" — easy to misread as "you'll get a second prompt". You did the right thing for the wrong contract.

**Changes for 0.37.1** (single-step, post-ship polish on the `0.37.0` push arc):

- `src/push.rs` — template header rewritten with your wording: contract stated up front, both auto-insertions (separator blank line + ochid trailer) called out so the user knows not to type either.
- `src/push.rs` — `parse_message` relaxed: first non-comment line = title, remainder = body. Blank line between is now allowed but not required (the commit-stage `format!` calls already insert the separator). This closes the silent footgun where typing title-then-body with no blank between made the *whole thing* the title.
- `src/push.rs` — two new `parse_message_cases` tests: no-blank-line case, multi-paragraph body with internal blank lines.
- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — `0.37.0` → `0.37.1`.
- `notes/chores-05.md` — new `## Tighten $EDITOR template wording (0.37.1)` subsection with the why + per-file edit list + rationale for single-step over `-N` continuation.
- `notes/todo.md` — Done entry + reference `[53]`.

Pre-commit checklist green: fmt clean, clippy clean, 202 tests pass, install + `vc-x1 --version` reports `0.37.1`.

Work complete. Please review. On approval I'll prep the commit title and body for a second approval before pushing.

content.content:
554	### test-fixture
555	
556	Scaffold a throwaway dual-repo jj workspace + local bare-git remotes
557	for testing `finalize` (and other subcommands) without touching live
558	workspace repos. Mirrors the real `vc-x1 init` layout minus the GitHub
559	side and the `~/.claude/projects/` symlink. Both repos get a described
560	initial commit with matching `ochid:` trailers, a tracked `main`
561	bookmark, and a pushed remote — so `finalize --push` flows work
562	end-to-end on either side.
563	
564	**Local remotes, not GitHub.** Each `origin` points at a bare-git
565	directory alongside the work trees (`<base>/remote-code.git/`,
566	`<base>/remote-claude.git/`) — no network, no auth, no GitHub. Pushes
567	succeed against these local bare repos and stay inside the fixture,
568	so nothing leaks out and nothing needs cleanup on a remote service.
569	When you're done, `vc-x1 test-fixture-rm <base>` wipes the whole
570	thing.
571	
572	```bash
573	vc-x1 test-fixture                 # base = $TMPDIR/vc-x1-test-<timestamp>
574	vc-x1 test-fixture --path /tmp/t1  # explicit path
575	vc-x1 test-fixture --use-template ../vc-template-x1  # seed from sibling templates
576	```
577	
578	`--use-template` takes the same `CODE[,BOT]` value as `vc-x1 init`
579	(bot defaults to `<CODE>.claude` sibling). Non-hidden template contents
580	are copied into `work/` and `work/.claude/`, and each repo's
581	`README.md` first line is rewritten to `# work` / `# work.claude`.
582	This is the path for eyeballing the template-copy result without
583	hitting GitHub.
584	
585	Layout:
586	```
587	<base>/
588	  remote-code.git/     bare git remote for code repo
589	  remote-claude.git/   bare git remote for .claude session repo
590	  work/                code repo (jj colocated, main tracks origin)
591	    .vc-config.toml    path="/",       other-repo=".claude"
592	    .gitignore         /.claude /.git /.jj /target
593	    .claude/           session repo (jj colocated, main tracks origin)
594	      .vc-config.toml  path="/.claude", other-repo=".."
595	      .gitignore       .git .jj
596	```
597	
598	Example — running `vc-x1 test-fixture` (the timestamp suffix varies):
599	```
600	$ vc-x1 test-fixture
601	Creating test fixture at /tmp/vc-x1-test-8PD4x8
602	Step 1: Initializing bare git remotes...
603	Step 2: Initializing work repo (jj colocated)...
604	Initialized repo in "."
605	Hint: Running `git clean -xdf` will remove `.jj/`!
606	Step 3: Initializing .claude session repo (jj colocated)...
607	Initialized repo in "."
608	Hint: Running `git clean -xdf` will remove `.jj/`!
609	Step 4: Initial commits with placeholder ochids...
610	Working copy  (@) now at: znkwzomz 0ce34d9b (empty) (no description set)
611	Parent commit (@-)      : xoknyroz 93ca19eb initial commit
612	Working copy  (@) now at: kmtpuzox f8c9b5af (empty) (no description set)
613	Parent commit (@-)      : mwutluxv 40438e05 initial commit
614	Step 5: Setting ochid cross-references...
615	Rebased 1 descendant commits
616	Step 6: Setting bookmarks and wiring remotes...
617	Created 1 bookmarks pointing to xoknyroz 46b9cd88 main | initial commit
618	Created 1 bookmarks pointing to mwutluxv 31a49010 main | initial commit
619	Step 7: Pushing main to both remotes...
620	Changes to push to origin:
621	  Add bookmark main to 46b9cd886e64
622	Changes to push to origin:
623	  Add bookmark main to 31a490102db7
624	
625	Fixture ready (local bare-git remotes, see README.md § test-fixture):
626	  Code repo:     /tmp/vc-x1-test-8PD4x8/work
627	  Session repo:  /tmp/vc-x1-test-8PD4x8/work/.claude
628	  Code remote:   /tmp/vc-x1-test-8PD4x8/remote-code.git
629	  Claude remote: /tmp/vc-x1-test-8PD4x8/remote-claude.git
630	
631	Next steps — see README.md § Testing push + finalize for the full flow.
632	Quick reference with this fixture's paths:
633	  jj git push -R /tmp/vc-x1-test-8PD4x8/work
634	  vc-x1 finalize --repo /tmp/vc-x1-test-8PD4x8/work/.claude --squash --push main --detach
635	  vc-x1 test-fixture-rm /tmp/vc-x1-test-8PD4x8
636	```
637	
638	### Testing push + finalize
639	
640	Always test against a throwaway fixture, never the live workspace.
641	Scaffold one with `test-fixture` (above), then run the complete
642	push + finalize flow end-to-end. The code repo uses plain
643	`jj git push`; the session repo uses `vc-x1 finalize` to squash
644	trailing writes and push in one shot.
645	
646	```bash
647	base=$(mktemp -u /tmp/vc-x1-test-XXXXXX)
648	vc-x1 test-fixture --path "$base"
649	work="$base/work"
650	session="$base/work/.claude"
651	
652	# 1. code repo: described commit → advance main → push
653	echo hello > "$work/hello.txt"
654	jj describe @ -R "$work" -m 'feat: add hello.txt'
655	jj bookmark set main -r @ -R "$work"
656	jj git push -R "$work"
657	
658	# 2. session repo: trailing writes → finalize (squash into @-, push)
659	echo notes > "$session/notes.md"
660	vc-x1 finalize --repo "$session" --squash --push main --detach \
661	    --log "$session/finalize.log"
662	
663	# 3. inspect the detached child's log once it's done (≈10s by default)
664	sleep 12 && cat "$session/finalize.log"
665	
666	# 4. cleanup when done
667	vc-x1 test-fixture-rm "$base"
668	```
669	
670	**Why `jj git push` for code but `finalize` for `.claude`?** The
671	code repo's workflow is a plain dev commit on `@-` that we push
672	directly. The session repo mirrors the bot's runtime pattern:
673	session writes land in `@` (above the last committed dev commit),

file.content:
### test-fixture

Scaffold a throwaway dual-repo jj workspace + local bare-git remotes
for testing `finalize` (and other subcommands) without touching live
workspace repos. Mirrors the real `vc-x1 init` layout minus the GitHub
side and the `~/.claude/projects/` symlink. Both repos get a described
initial commit with matching `ochid:` trailers, a tracked `main`
bookmark, and a pushed remote — so `finalize --push` flows work
end-to-end on either side.

**Local remotes, not GitHub.** Each `origin` points at a bare-git
directory alongside the work trees (`<base>/remote-code.git/`,
`<base>/remote-claude.git/`) — no network, no auth, no GitHub. Pushes
succeed against these local bare repos and stay inside the fixture,
so nothing leaks out and nothing needs cleanup on a remote service.
When you're done, `vc-x1 test-fixture-rm <base>` wipes the whole
thing.

```bash
vc-x1 test-fixture                 # base = $TMPDIR/vc-x1-test-<timestamp>
vc-x1 test-fixture --path /tmp/t1  # explicit path
vc-x1 test-fixture --use-template ../vc-template-x1  # seed from sibling templates
```

`--use-template` takes the same `CODE[,BOT]` value as `vc-x1 init`
(bot defaults to `<CODE>.claude` sibling). Non-hidden template contents
are copied into `work/` and `work/.claude/`, and each repo's
`README.md` first line is rewritten to `# work` / `# work.claude`.
This is the path for eyeballing the template-copy result without
hitting GitHub.

Layout:
```
<base>/
  remote-code.git/     bare git remote for code repo
  remote-claude.git/   bare git remote for .claude session repo
  work/                code repo (jj colocated, main tracks origin)
    .vc-config.toml    path="/",       other-repo=".claude"
    .gitignore         /.claude /.git /.jj /target
    .claude/           session repo (jj colocated, main tracks origin)
      .vc-config.toml  path="/.claude", other-repo=".."
      .gitignore       .git .jj
```

### Testing push + finalize

Always test against a throwaway fixture, never the live workspace.
Scaffold one with `test-fixture` (above), then run the complete
push + finalize flow end-to-end. The code repo uses plain
`jj git push`; the session repo uses `vc-x1 finalize` to squash
trailing writes and push in one shot.

```bash
base=$(mktemp -u /tmp/vc-x1-test-XXXXXX)
vc-x1 test-fixture --path "$base"
work="$base/work"
session="$base/work/.claude"

# 1. code repo: described commit → advance main → push
echo hello > "$work/hello.txt"
jj describe @ -R "$work" -m 'feat: add hello.txt'
jj bookmark set main -r @ -R "$work"
jj git push -R "$work"

# 2. session repo: trailing writes → finalize (squash into @-, push)
echo notes > "$session/notes.md"
vc-x1 finalize --repo "$session" --squash --push main --detach \
    --log "$session/finalize.log"

# 3. inspect the detached child's log once it's done (≈10s by default)
sleep 12 && cat "$session/finalize.log"

# 4. cleanup when done
vc-x1 test-fixture-rm "$base"
```

**Why `jj git push` for code but `finalize` for `.claude`?** The
code repo's workflow is a plain dev commit on `@-` that we push
directly. The session repo mirrors the bot's runtime pattern:
session writes land in `@` (above the last committed dev commit),

Yes — your assumption is right. Concrete recipe:

```bash
# 1. Scaffold the fixture (use --path or let it pick a tmp path)
vc-x1 test-fixture --path ./tf-1
cd ./tf-1/work

# 2. Make a change in the code repo
echo hello > hello.txt

# 3. Optional but realistic — also touch the session repo so the
#    .claude side has something to commit (the fixture has matching
#    main bookmarks + remotes on both sides)
echo session > .claude/session.txt

# 4. Run interactive push
vc-x1 push main

# 5. When done
cd /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1   # or wherever
vc-x1 test-fixture-rm ./tf-1         # path is relative to whichever cwd you run it from
```

What you'll see:

1. **Preflight** — sync + fmt + clippy + test. ⚠️ The fixture has no `Cargo.toml` so cargo steps won't fire as in real use; preflight will exit early on whichever check it can't run. (The integration tests pass `--no-finalize` and skip the cargo bits via test plumbing — see `src/push.rs:1660`.) You may need to use `--from review` or `--from message` to skip past preflight when dogfooding the editor flow specifically. Worth verifying.
2. **Review gate** — diff stats printed, `[y/N]` prompt.
3. **Message gate** — `$EDITOR` opens on `tf-1/work/.vc-x1/push-message.txt`. Test: type only a title and `:wq` → should now produce title with empty body (used to produce title-with-stuck-body); type title + body with no blank line → should split correctly (the bug fix).
4. **Gitignore warning** — fixture's `.gitignore` doesn't list `.vc-x1`, so a non-fatal warning will fire. Expected; ignore it.

There **isn't** a documented `vc-x1 push` test recipe — README §"Testing push + finalize" still uses the pre-`push` manual flow (`jj git push` + `vc-x1 finalize`). Worth adding a §"Testing push interactively" subsection if you find this recipe useful — say the word and I'll fold it in (still as part of 0.37.1, since it's directly motivated by the same dogfood that surfaced the template wording).

One thing to verify before you run: whether preflight's cargo steps are skippable on a fixture without a Cargo.toml, or whether they'll block the test. Want me to check first?

input.description: Look at sync exit code semantics

content.text:
Strong agree. `sync` mutating jj state inside preflight, before the user has even seen the review gate, is exactly the kind of surprise we should avoid.

Implementation question: dry-run sync currently prints status and returns `Ok(())` regardless of state — there's no exit signal saying "action needed." So I'd add a small flag:

**`vc-x1 sync --check`** — implies dry-run + treats `Behind`/`Diverged` as fatal. Conflicts with `--no-dry-run`. Push's preflight calls `vc-x1 sync --check`. Error message points the user at the manual fix:

```
error: sync: 1 repo needs action (see above) — resolve with `vc-x1 sync --no-dry-run` and re-run push
```

Output ordering preserved: per-repo state lines from `log_state` print first (so the user sees *which* repo + state), then the error.

Naming alternatives: `--strict`, `--fail-if-action`. I prefer `--check` — short, reads naturally ("just check, don't act, fail if not clean"), matches the common CLI convention (`cargo check`, `gofmt -l` style).

So 0.37.1 grows to four changes, all from the same first-dogfood session:

1. Template wording
2. Parser relax
3. Gitignore: fatal in push, auto-write in init + test-fixture
4. Sync preflight: dry-run + fatal via new `vc-x1 sync --check`

I think keeping them as one 0.37.1 commit is right — chores entry framing ("first dogfood polish") covers all of them and the diff stays scannable. Alternative: split into 0.37.1 (template+parser, already done) and 0.37.2 (gitignore+sync) so the editor fix isn't held up by the bigger change.

Your call on bundle vs split. Then I proceed with both #3 and #4.

I suggest we add both flags and --check is the default but I like the notion of being specific in in scripts and not "assuming" defaults, you do that, but I as the user do use defaults. So "I can assume" defaults but "auto" tests shouldn't and "bots" are auto tests, thoughts?

content.text:
Strong agree on the philosophy — explicit-in-scripts is a discipline that pays off when defaults shift. CLAUDE.md should use the explicit forms too since the bot is automation.

To confirm the naming/scope before I code:

**`src/sync.rs`** — replace the `--dry-run` / `--no-dry-run` pair with `--check` / `--no-check`. Default = `--check`. Semantics:
- `--check` (default): fetch, classify, print state, **error** if any repo is `Behind`/`Diverged`. Non-zero exit on action-needed.
- `--no-check`: fetch, classify, print, **apply** (rebase / fast-forward).

This is a breaking CLI change — `vc-x1 sync --no-dry-run` stops working. Since this is the user's tool and the change is small, I think replace > alias-with-deprecation. Worth flagging though.

**`src/push.rs`** preflight — explicit `vc-x1 sync --check` (not relying on default, per your "bots use explicit flags" rule).

**Docs** to update:
- `CLAUDE.md` (both copies — app and `.claude` per your dual-repo workflow): "Pre-step: `vc-x1 sync`" section + every `--no-dry-run` mention.
- `README.md`: sync usage examples.
- `notes/chores-05.md`: extend the 0.37.1 entry.
- `notes/todo.md`: done-line text.

**Tests**: sync's tests reference `no_dry_run` field — rename through. Push tests + integration tests don't hit sync directly (they use `--from message`), so no impact there.

One alternative naming I'll mention but not push: `--check` / `--apply`. Reads more symmetric and `--apply` states the positive action. But `--check` / `--no-check` matches the existing clap negation-pair convention in this codebase (you've used `--no-finalize`, `--no-label`, etc.), so consistency-wise yours wins. Sticking with your call unless you say otherwise.

Green light?

message.content: --no-check fails on "all" errors?

content.text:
Same errors it fails on today — `--no-check` only flips the "action needed → error" gate off. Other failures still error:

- **Fetch failure** (network, auth, ref doesn't exist) → fatal, op-restored.
- **Rebase conflict** during apply → fatal, op-restored.
- **`jj` subprocess errors** of any kind → fatal, op-restored.

The `--check` vs `--no-check` axis only controls one thing: *is "this repo needs work" itself an error?* `--check` says yes (so the user has to consciously apply); `--no-check` says no (because applying is the whole point).

So the contract is:
- `--check`: clean → exit 0; action-needed → exit 1; anything actually broken → exit 1.
- `--no-check`: clean → exit 0; action-needed → apply (then exit 0 if successful); anything actually broken (incl. failed apply) → exit 1.

Did I read the question right?

I suggest "push xxx: blah" easier for me to parse and there a quite a few plus tool output it's quite noisy, and maybe we'll want to make it less so in the future. But I think the space might help me.

- `push:preflight:` → `push preflight:`
- `push:review:` → `push review:`

- `push:finalize-claude:` → `push finalize-claude:`
- `push:step:` → `push step:`

Bare `push: ...` lines (top-level, no stage) stay as-is — they're not the noisy steps you're seeing.

content.text:
Yes — doable cleanly. Subprocess output flows through `common::run` (one helper), and that's the single point to add indentation. Right now stderr (where jj prints `Working copy now at...` etc.) goes through `info!("{stderr}")` un-indented — a one-line change to per-line `info!("  {line}")` indents every subprocess line under whatever `push <stage>:` line preceded it.

Two notes:

1. **Scope is global, not push-only** — `common::run` is shared by every subcommand (sync, init, finalize, etc.), so all subprocess output gets the 2-space indent. That's almost certainly what you want (tidier output everywhere), but flagging since it's a change beyond just push's surface.

2. **`$EDITOR` is untouched** — push.rs:945 launches the editor with `.status()`, streaming directly to the terminal (vi needs that — can't pipe). So the editor session looks the same as today.

So 0.37.1 is now six changes:

1. Template wording
2. Parser relax
3. Gitignore: fatal in push, auto-write in init + test-fixture
4. Sync: `--check` / `--no-check` rename + fatal-on-action-needed in `--check`
5. Log prefix: `push:STAGE:` → `push STAGE:`
6. Subprocess output: indent stderr 2 spaces in `common::run`

Green light to start coding all of them?

lastPrompt: One last suggestion for now now, how hard would it be to make tool output optional and seen only with -v,--verbose, which we have according to the help for push?

content.text:
Easy — one line. Subprocess stderr currently goes through `info!` in `common::run`. Change to `debug!` and it's hidden by default, surfaced with `-v`. Bonus: `debug!` already indents per-line in the formatter (logging.rs:94-96), so this change **subsumes #6** — no separate indent edit needed.

But there's a scope question worth deciding before I code:

**`common::run` is shared across every subcommand.** Hiding subprocess stderr globally affects:

- `vc-x1 init` — silences `Initialized repo in "."` + `Hint: git clean -xdf will remove .jj/`. (User probably wants to see init's confirmation today.)
- `vc-x1 sync --no-check` — silences `Rebased N commits` from the rebase subprocess.
- `vc-x1 finalize` — silences `jj squash` + `jj git push` confirmations.
- `vc-x1 clone` — silences `jj git clone` progress.

For push, hiding is exactly what you want — high-level `push <stage>:` lines tell the story; jj's chatter is debug detail. For init/finalize/clone, the subprocess message often *is* the confirmation the user is waiting for.

Three choices:

1. **Global hide.** Simplest. After this lands, init/finalize/clone get quiet by default; their critical messages move to the subcommand's own `info!` calls if/when dogfooding shows they're missed. Trust-but-verify approach.
2. **Push-only.** Add a `quiet: bool` field to a new `RunOpts` (or a sibling `run_quiet` helper). Push opts in; everyone else stays loud. More code, no surprise behavior changes elsewhere.
3. **Default loud, opt-in quiet via env or config.** Heaviest, probably overkill for now.

My instinct: **(1) global hide**. It matches your earlier "less noisy" framing, and `-v` is a well-known escape hatch. If init feels too quiet, we add an explicit `info!("init: created <path>")` in init.rs at that moment — better UX than the raw jj passthrough anyway.

Your call. Once you pick, I start coding all six (well, five — #6 collapses).

41	/// Default repo set when `-R` isn't given — the canonical dual-repo pair.
42	const DEFAULT_REPOS: [&str; 2] = [".", ".claude"];
43	
44	/// Resolve the caller's `-R` flags into a concrete repo list.
45	///
46	/// Empty input (no `-R` given) falls back to `DEFAULT_REPOS`. Each
47	/// provided value is also split on `,` and trimmed, so
48	/// `-R .,.claude` works identically to `-R . -R .claude`.
49	fn resolve_repos(raw: &[PathBuf]) -> Vec<PathBuf> {
50	    if raw.is_empty() {
51	        return DEFAULT_REPOS.iter().map(PathBuf::from).collect();
52	    }
53	    raw.iter()
54	        .flat_map(|p| {
55	            p.to_string_lossy()
56	                .split(',')
57	                .map(|s| PathBuf::from(s.trim()))
58	                .collect::<Vec<_>>()
59	        })
60	        .collect()
61	}

73	    Diverged { local: String, remote: String },
74	    /// The bookmark has no `@<remote>` counterpart.
75	    NoRemote,

243	/// Ensure `@` is a descendant of `bookmark`, rebasing if not.
244	///
245	/// `jj git fetch` fast-forwards a tracked local bookmark to the remote
246	/// tip when local is a strict ancestor, but leaves `@` behind — still
247	/// parented on the *pre-fetch* bookmark commit. The `.claude` repo is
248	/// the motivating case: trailing session writes (e.g. `/exit`'s jsonl
249	/// tail) sit in `@`, and without this step they'd end up orphaned on a
250	/// stale branch when the remote advanced.
251	///
252	/// Skipped when `main::@` is already non-empty (i.e., `@` is already
253	/// reachable from `bookmark`). On `--no-dry-run`, runs
254	/// `jj rebase -b @ -d <bookmark>` which carries all commits between
255	/// the old parent and `@` forward, then checks `conflicts()` — any
256	/// conflict in this step trips the outer revert the same way a
257	/// conflicted `act_on_state` rebase would.
258	fn ensure_at_on_main(
259	    repo: &Path,
260	    bookmark: &str,
261	    apply: bool,
262	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
263	    if revset_nonempty(repo, &format!("{bookmark}::@"))? {
264	        return Ok(());
265	    }
266	    info!("{}: rebasing @ onto '{bookmark}'", repo.display());
267	    if apply {
268	        run(
269	            "jj",
270	            &["rebase", "-b", "@", "-d", bookmark, "-R", &repo_str(repo)],
271	            Path::new("."),
272	        )?;
273	        if has_conflicts(repo)? {
274	            return Err(format!(
275	                "{}: rebase of @ onto '{bookmark}' produced conflicts",
276	                repo.display()
277	            )
278	            .into());
279	        }
280	    }
281	    Ok(())
282	}

content.content:
405	On any failure — conflicted rebase, subprocess error, anything — `sync`
406	restores every repo to its starting state via `jj op restore`. Either
407	every repo advances or none do. Working-copy files are preserved
408	across the revert: jj rewinds the operation log but leaves disk
409	content untouched, and any conflicted commits introduced by the failed
410	rebase are abandoned on the way back.
411	
412	```
413	vc-x1 sync                            # dual-repo default, dry-run
414	vc-x1 sync --no-dry-run               # dual-repo, act
415	vc-x1 sync -R .                       # single-repo project
416	vc-x1 sync -R .,.claude -R ../other   # mixed: repeat + comma-separate
417	```
418	
419	| Flag | Description |
420	|------|-------------|
421	| `-R, --repo <PATH>` | Repo to sync; repeatable or comma-separated [default: `.,.claude`] |
422	| `--no-dry-run` | Apply; without it, classify and report only |
423	| `-q, --quiet` | Suppress all output; exit code signals result (for scripts) |
424	| `--bookmark <NAME>` | Bookmark to sync in each repo [default: main] |
425	| `--remote <NAME>` | Remote to sync against [default: origin] |
426	
427	**Output shape.** Sync collapses output based on what it finds:
428	
429	- **All up-to-date** — one-line summary:
430	  `sync: N repos, all up-to-date`. Nothing else. Makes "sprinkle
431	  sync everywhere" genuinely cheap.
432	- **Action needed** (`behind` / `diverged`) — per-repo fetch +
433	  state lines, then `dry-run — re-run with --no-dry-run to apply`
434	  (or the actual actions on `--no-dry-run`).
435	- **`--quiet`** — no output at any level; exit code is the only
436	  signal. Intended for scripts that just need success/failure.
437	
438	**Note on the `behind` case.** jj's `git fetch` already fast-forwards a
439	tracked local bookmark when it's a strict ancestor of the incoming
440	remote, so in the common case `sync` reports `up-to-date` rather than
441	`behind`. The `behind` branch covers untracked bookmarks and edge
442	configs where auto-advance is disabled.
443	
444	### finalize
445	
446	Atomically squash, set bookmark, and push a jj repo. The primary use
447	case is the bot finalizing its own session repo (`.claude`) at the end
448	of a session. The bot can't just `jj commit` because the act of
449	committing generates more session data — files written after the commit
450	would be lost. `finalize` solves this by:
451	
452	1. **`--detach`**: spawning a background process so the bot session can
453	   end immediately (no more writes after the bot exits)
454	2. **`--delay`**: waiting for trailing writes to settle

file.content:
On any failure — conflicted rebase, subprocess error, anything — `sync`
restores every repo to its starting state via `jj op restore`. Either
every repo advances or none do. Working-copy files are preserved
across the revert: jj rewinds the operation log but leaves disk
content untouched, and any conflicted commits introduced by the failed
rebase are abandoned on the way back.

```
vc-x1 sync                            # dual-repo default, dry-run
vc-x1 sync --no-dry-run               # dual-repo, act
vc-x1 sync -R .                       # single-repo project
vc-x1 sync -R .,.claude -R ../other   # mixed: repeat + comma-separate
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `-R, --repo <PATH>` | Repo to sync; repeatable or comma-separated [default: `.,.claude`] |
| `--no-dry-run` | Apply; without it, classify and report only |
| `-q, --quiet` | Suppress all output; exit code signals result (for scripts) |
| `--bookmark <NAME>` | Bookmark to sync in each repo [default: main] |
| `--remote <NAME>` | Remote to sync against [default: origin] |

**Output shape.** Sync collapses output based on what it finds:

- **All up-to-date** — one-line summary:
  `sync: N repos, all up-to-date`. Nothing else. Makes "sprinkle
  sync everywhere" genuinely cheap.
- **Action needed** (`behind` / `diverged`) — per-repo fetch +
  state lines, then `dry-run — re-run with --no-dry-run to apply`
  (or the actual actions on `--no-dry-run`).
- **`--quiet`** — no output at any level; exit code is the only
  signal. Intended for scripts that just need success/failure.

**Note on the `behind` case.** jj's `git fetch` already fast-forwards a
tracked local bookmark when it's a strict ancestor of the incoming
remote, so in the common case `sync` reports `up-to-date` rather than
`behind`. The `behind` branch covers untracked bookmarks and edge
configs where auto-advance is disabled.

### finalize

Atomically squash, set bookmark, and push a jj repo. The primary use
case is the bot finalizing its own session repo (`.claude`) at the end
of a session. The bot can't just `jj commit` because the act of
committing generates more session data — files written after the commit
would be lost. `finalize` solves this by:

1. **`--detach`**: spawning a background process so the bot session can
   end immediately (no more writes after the bot exits)
2. **`--delay`**: waiting for trailing writes to settle

input.new_string:
```
vc-x1 sync                            # dual-repo default, --check (verify)
vc-x1 sync --check                    # explicit form of the default
vc-x1 sync --no-check                 # dual-repo, apply
vc-x1 sync -R .                       # single-repo project
vc-x1 sync -R .,.claude -R ../other   # mixed: repeat + comma-separate
```

Scripts and automation should pass `--check` or `--no-check`
explicitly rather than rely on the default — defaults can shift,
explicit flags lock in the contract. Interactive use can take the
default.

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `-R, --repo <PATH>` | Repo to sync; repeatable or comma-separated [default: `.,.claude`] |
| `--check` | Verify only — fetch + classify, error if any repo needs action (default) |
| `--no-check` | Apply — fetch + classify, then rebase/fast-forward as needed |
| `-q, --quiet` | Suppress all output; exit code signals result (for scripts) |
| `--bookmark <NAME>` | Bookmark to sync in each repo [default: main] |
| `--remote <NAME>` | Remote to sync against [default: origin] |

**Output shape.** Sync collapses output based on what it finds:

- **All up-to-date** — one-line summary:
  `sync: N repos, all up-to-date`. Nothing else. Makes "sprinkle
  sync everywhere" genuinely cheap.
- **Action needed** (`behind` / `diverged`) — per-repo fetch +
  state lines, then **fatal in `--check` mode** with
  `sync: N repos need action — resolve with vc-x1 sync --no-check
  and re-run`. Under `--no-check`, the actions run instead.
- **`--quiet`** — no output at any level; exit code is the only
  signal. Intended for scripts that just need success/failure.

### Pre-step: `vc-x1 sync` (still useful)

Push's preflight runs `vc-x1 sync --no-dry-run` as its first step
so divergence is resolved before the build. Running sync manually
before you *start* editing is still cheap (one line when clean)
and surfaces remote changes earlier:

```
vc-x1 sync                            # dual-repo workspace
vc-x1 sync -R .                       # single-repo project
vc-x1 sync --quiet                    # silent; exit code signals result
```

Output shape:

- **Clean**: one line — `sync: N repos, all up-to-date`. Proceed.
- **Action needed** (`behind` / `diverged`): per-repo fetch +
  state lines, then `dry-run — re-run with --no-dry-run to apply`.
  Inspect, then re-run with `--no-dry-run` (or resolve conflicts
  if rebase fails).
- **`--quiet`**: no output; exit code is the only signal.

### After finalize: stop and wait

input.new_string:
### Pre-step: `vc-x1 sync` (still useful)

Push's preflight runs `vc-x1 sync --check` as its first step. Check
mode verifies divergence without applying — if anything is `behind`
or `diverged`, preflight errors and you resolve explicitly with
`vc-x1 sync --no-check` before re-running push. This keeps the
two approval gates (review, message) authoritative; push never
performs an unsupervised rebase.

Running sync manually before you *start* editing is still cheap
(one line when clean) and surfaces remote changes earlier:

```
vc-x1 sync --check                    # dual-repo workspace, verify
vc-x1 sync --no-check                 # apply (rebase / fast-forward)
vc-x1 sync --check -R .               # single-repo project
vc-x1 sync --check --quiet            # silent; exit code signals result
```

Bots and scripts must pass `--check` or `--no-check` explicitly —
defaults can shift, explicit flags lock in the contract. Interactive
use can take the default (which is `--check`).

Output shape:

- **Clean**: one line — `sync: N repos, all up-to-date`. Proceed.
- **Action needed** (`behind` / `diverged`) under `--check`:
  per-repo fetch + state lines, then a fatal
  `sync: N repos need action — resolve with vc-x1 sync --no-check
  and re-run`. Inspect, run `vc-x1 sync --no-check`, then proceed.
- **`--quiet`**: no output; exit code is the only signal.

file.content:
## Tighten `$EDITOR` template wording (0.37.1)

First dogfood of the interactive `vc-x1 push` flow exposed two
papercuts in the editor template:

1. The header was structured "title on first line / blank line /
   body after" — easy to read as "title now, body in a second
   prompt later". A user typed only the title, `:wq`'d, and was
   surprised when push proceeded with an empty body instead of
   re-prompting.
2. The template required a literal blank line between title and
   body. If a user typed title-then-body with no blank between,
   `parse_message`'s `splitn(2, "\n\n")` made the *whole thing*
   the title and the body empty — silent footgun.

The fix is to make the template's contract explicit and to relax
the parser so the blank-line separator is not user-required.

- `src/push.rs` — template header rewritten:

  > Leave as-is to abort. Enter the Title on the first line,
  > optionally followed by the Body on subsequent lines. The
  > blank line between Title and Body is inserted automatically,
  > and the ochid trailer is appended per-repo automatically —
  > don't add either here.
  > Lines starting with `#` are ignored.

  States the contract up front (one editor session, title on
  line 1, body optional after) and surfaces the two
  auto-insertions (separator blank line, ochid trailer) so the
  user knows not to type either.

- `src/push.rs` — `parse_message` rewritten: first non-comment
  line is the title, remainder (trimmed) is the body. Blank
  line between is allowed but not required. Doc comment updated.
  The "blank line between title and body in the final commit
  message" responsibility lives in the commit-stage `format!`
  calls (`"{title}\n\n{body}\n\nochid: ..."`), which were
  already there — so no change needed downstream of the parser.

- `src/push.rs` — `parse_message_cases` test grows two cases:
  title+body with no blank line (new behavior), and a body
  with internal blank lines preserved (regression guard for
  the multi-paragraph body case the old splitn-based code
  also handled correctly but is now exercised by a different
  code path).

- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — version `0.37.0` → `0.37.1`.

- `notes/todo.md` — Done entry added.

**Why single-step `0.37.1` rather than `0.37.0-6`:** the 0.37.0
arc was closed by the done marker; this is a focused mechanical
fix discovered during first-real-use, not a continuation of the
push design work. Keeps the dev ladder of a feature distinct
from post-ship polish.

input.new_string:
## First-dogfood polish for push (0.37.1)

First dogfood of the interactive `vc-x1 push` flow exposed six
papercuts. They're all small, all surfaced in the same session, and
all share the same "post-ship polish" framing — so they ship as one
single-step `0.37.1` rather than a `0.37.0-6` continuation. The
0.37.0 arc was closed by the done marker; this keeps the dev ladder
of the feature distinct from sandpaper.

### 1. `$EDITOR` template wording + parser relaxation

The header was structured "title on first line / blank line / body
after" — easy to read as "title now, body in a second prompt later".
A user typed only the title, `:wq`'d, and was surprised when push
proceeded with an empty body instead of re-prompting. The template
also required a literal blank line between title and body — if a
user typed title-then-body with no blank between, `parse_message`'s
`splitn(2, "\n\n")` made the *whole thing* the title. Silent
footgun.

- `src/push.rs` — template header rewritten:

  > Leave as-is to abort. Enter the Title on the first line,
  > optionally followed by the Body on subsequent lines. The
  > blank line between Title and Body is inserted automatically,
  > and the ochid trailer is appended per-repo automatically —
  > don't add either here.
  > Lines starting with `#` are ignored.

  States the contract up front (one editor session, title on line
  1, body optional after) and surfaces the two auto-insertions
  (separator blank line, ochid trailer) so the user knows not to
  type either.

- `src/push.rs` — `parse_message` rewritten: first non-comment
  line = title, remainder (trimmed) = body. Blank line between is
  allowed but not required. The "blank line between title and body
  in the final commit message" responsibility lives in the
  commit-stage `format!` calls (`"{title}\n\n{body}\n\nochid: ..."`),
  which were already there.

- `src/push.rs` — `parse_message_cases` grows two new cases:
  title+body with no blank line (new behavior), and a multi-line
  body with internal blank lines (regression guard).

### 2. Gitignore coherence: fatal in push, auto-write in init + fixture

The gitignore-coherence check was a `warn!` — easy to miss in the
preflight wall of output. A committed push-state file is a real
foot-gun (jj would track session-only state into history). Promote
to fatal, and have `init` and `test-fixture` ship `/.vc-x1` in their
generated `.gitignore` so the fresh-repo path is clean.

- `src/push.rs` — `check_gitignore_coherence` returns
  `Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>` and `push_in` propagates with `?`.
  Error message names the file and the line to add. No bypass
  flag; the fix is one line.

- `src/init.rs` — `GITIGNORE_CODE` adds `/.vc-x1`; matching test
  asserts the new line.

- `src/test_fixture.rs` — `GITIGNORE_CODE` adds `/.vc-x1`; matching
  test asserts the new line. Push integration tests use this
  fixture, so the now-fatal check passes there transparently.

- `README.md` — fixture-layout snippet adds `/.vc-x1`.

### 3. `vc-x1 sync`: `--check` / `--no-check` rename + fatal check mode

Push's preflight ran `vc-x1 sync --no-dry-run`, which auto-rebased
the local repo against the remote *before* the user reached the
review/message gates. A surprise rebase mid-push is exactly the
kind of unsupervised mutation the gates are meant to prevent.

Rename `--no-dry-run` to `--no-check` and `--dry-run` to `--check`
(the implicit default; passable explicitly). Check mode is now
**fatal** when any repo is `behind`/`diverged` — the user must
resolve with `vc-x1 sync --no-check` and re-run. Push's preflight
calls `vc-x1 sync --check` so divergence surfaces early but never
rewrites local state without explicit consent.

The bot uses explicit forms in scripts and automation (per
"defaults can shift, explicit flags lock in the contract"); the
interactive user can rely on the default.

- `src/sync.rs` — `SyncArgs.no_dry_run: bool` → two fields
  `check: bool` and `no_check: bool`, mutually `conflicts_with`.
  Field `args.no_dry_run` → `args.no_check` everywhere internally
  (semantic preserved: true = act). New phase-4 logic: in check
  mode (`!args.no_check`) with `any_action_needed`, return Err
  with explicit "resolve with `vc-x1 sync --no-check` and re-run"
  message and a per-repo state recap above (already printed by
  phase 2).

- `src/sync.rs` — `parse_defaults` / `parse_overrides` tests
  updated; new `parse_check_flag` and `parse_check_no_check_conflict`
  tests exercise the new flag surface.

- `src/sync.rs` — `apply_args()` test helper updated to
  `{ check: false, no_check: true, ... }`.

- `src/push.rs` — `stage_preflight` calls `vc-x1 sync --check`
  (was `--no-dry-run`). Doc comment rewritten to explain why
  check mode (no auto-rebase before the gates).

- `CLAUDE.md` — "Pre-step: `vc-x1 sync` (still useful)" section
  rewritten around `--check`/`--no-check`. Output-shape bullets
  updated to describe the fatal check-mode error.

- `README.md` — sync usage examples + flag table + output-shape
  section updated. Push-stage table's `preflight` row updated to
  `vc-x1 sync --check`.

### 4. Stage-prefix log format: `push:STAGE:` → `push STAGE:`

Stage-tagged log lines were `push:preflight:`, `push:review:`,
… `push:finalize-claude:`. With nine prefixes plus subprocess
output, the colon-pair felt dense; a single space between "push"
and the stage parses faster.

- `src/push.rs` — nine stage prefixes renamed (preflight,
  review, message, commit-app, commit-claude, bookmark-both,
  push-app, finalize-claude, step). Bare `push: ...` lines
  (top-level, no stage) are unchanged.

  Side note: `push push-app:` now reads with the word "push"
  twice in a row. The stage's name is `push-app` because its
  job is to push the app repo; renaming it would touch the
  state-file format and `--from` values, which is wider surgery
  than warranted here.

### 5. Subprocess output hidden by default (visible with `-v`)

Subprocess stderr (jj's `Working copy now at ...`, `Rebased N
commits`, etc.) was emitted at `info!` in `common::run`, so it
showed by default. Combined with the dense `push:STAGE:` prefix
and the multi-stage flow, the output felt noisy. Drop subprocess
stderr to `debug!` — hidden by default, surfaced with `-v`. The
debug formatter already indents per-line two spaces, so when `-v`
is set, subprocess lines sit visually under the caller's `info!`
header. This change subsumes the originally-planned "indent
stderr two spaces in info!" fix.

- `src/common.rs` — `run` doc comment rewritten. Subprocess
  stderr success path: `info!("{stderr}")` → `debug!("{stderr}")`.
  Subprocess stdout: `debug!("  {stdout}")` → `debug!("{stdout}")`
  (drop the redundant inline indent — the formatter already
  indents debug lines).

  Scope note: `common::run` is shared by every subcommand, so this
  silences subprocess output globally (init, sync, finalize, clone,
  push). Trade-off accepted: high-volume jj chatter is rarely
  load-bearing for the user, and `-v` brings it back. If a specific
  subcommand needs a particular subprocess line surfaced, the right
  fix is an explicit `info!` in that subcommand at the moment the
  thing happens — not relying on subprocess passthrough.

### 6. Versioning + bookkeeping

- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — version `0.37.0` → `0.37.1`.
- `notes/todo.md` — Done entry added (covers all 5 fixes).

  the multi-paragraph body case the old splitn-based code
  also handled correctly but is now exercised by a different
  code path).

- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — version `0.37.0` → `0.37.1`.

- `notes/todo.md` — Done entry added.

**Why single-step `0.37.1` rather than `0.37.0-6`:** the 0.37.0
arc was closed by the done marker; this is a focused mechanical
fix discovered during first-real-use, not a continuation of the
push design work. Keeps the dev ladder of a feature distinct
from post-ship polish.
toolUseResult.newString:
## First-dogfood polish for push (0.37.1)

First dogfood of the interactive `vc-x1 push` flow exposed six
papercuts. They're all small, all surfaced in the same session, and
all share the same "post-ship polish" framing — so they ship as one
single-step `0.37.1` rather than a `0.37.0-6` continuation. The
0.37.0 arc was closed by the done marker; this keeps the dev ladder
of the feature distinct from sandpaper.

### 1. `$EDITOR` template wording + parser relaxation

The header was structured "title on first line / blank line / body
after" — easy to read as "title now, body in a second prompt later".
A user typed only the title, `:wq`'d, and was surprised when push
proceeded with an empty body instead of re-prompting. The template
also required a literal blank line between title and body — if a
user typed title-then-body with no blank between, `parse_message`'s
`splitn(2, "\n\n")` made the *whole thing* the title. Silent
footgun.

- `src/push.rs` — template header rewritten:

  > Leave as-is to abort. Enter the Title on the first line,
  > optionally followed by the Body on subsequent lines. The
  > blank line between Title and Body is inserted automatically,
  > and the ochid trailer is appended per-repo automatically —
  > don't add either here.
  > Lines starting with `#` are ignored.

  States the contract up front (one editor session, title on line
  1, body optional after) and surfaces the two auto-insertions
  (separator blank line, ochid trailer) so the user knows not to
  type either.

- `src/push.rs` — `parse_message` rewritten: first non-comment
  line = title, remainder (trimmed) = body. Blank line between is
  allowed but not required. The "blank line between title and body
  in the final commit message" responsibility lives in the
  commit-stage `format!` calls (`"{title}\n\n{body}\n\nochid: ..."`),
  which were already there.

- `src/push.rs` — `parse_message_cases` grows two new cases:
  title+body with no blank line (new behavior), and a multi-line
  body with internal blank lines (regression guard).

### 2. Gitignore coherence: fatal in push, auto-write in init + fixture

The gitignore-coherence check was a `warn!` — easy to miss in the
preflight wall of output. A committed push-state file is a real
foot-gun (jj would track session-only state into history). Promote
to fatal, and have `init` and `test-fixture` ship `/.vc-x1` in their
generated `.gitignore` so the fresh-repo path is clean.

- `src/push.rs` — `check_gitignore_coherence` returns
  `Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>` and `push_in` propagates with `?`.
  Error message names the file and the line to add. No bypass
  flag; the fix is one line.

- `src/init.rs` — `GITIGNORE_CODE` adds `/.vc-x1`; matching test
  asserts the new line.

- `src/test_fixture.rs` — `GITIGNORE_CODE` adds `/.vc-x1`; matching
  test asserts the new line. Push integration tests use this
  fixture, so the now-fatal check passes there transparently.

- `README.md` — fixture-layout snippet adds `/.vc-x1`.

### 3. `vc-x1 sync`: `--check` / `--no-check` rename + fatal check mode

Push's preflight ran `vc-x1 sync --no-dry-run`, which auto-rebased
the local repo against the remote *before* the user reached the
review/message gates. A surprise rebase mid-push is exactly the
kind of unsupervised mutation the gates are meant to prevent.

Rename `--no-dry-run` to `--no-check` and `--dry-run` to `--check`
(the implicit default; passable explicitly). Check mode is now
**fatal** when any repo is `behind`/`diverged` — the user must
resolve with `vc-x1 sync --no-check` and re-run. Push's preflight
calls `vc-x1 sync --check` so divergence surfaces early but never
rewrites local state without explicit consent.

The bot uses explicit forms in scripts and automation (per
"defaults can shift, explicit flags lock in the contract"); the
interactive user can rely on the default.

- `src/sync.rs` — `SyncArgs.no_dry_run: bool` → two fields
  `check: bool` and `no_check: bool`, mutually `conflicts_with`.
  Field `args.no_dry_run` → `args.no_check` everywhere internally
  (semantic preserved: true = act). New phase-4 logic: in check
  mode (`!args.no_check`) with `any_action_needed`, return Err
  with explicit "resolve with `vc-x1 sync --no-check` and re-run"
  message and a per-repo state recap above (already printed by
  phase 2).

- `src/sync.rs` — `parse_defaults` / `parse_overrides` tests
  updated; new `parse_check_flag` and `parse_check_no_check_conflict`
  tests exercise the new flag surface.

- `src/sync.rs` — `apply_args()` test helper updated to
  `{ check: false, no_check: true, ... }`.

- `src/push.rs` — `stage_preflight` calls `vc-x1 sync --check`
  (was `--no-dry-run`). Doc comment rewritten to explain why
  check mode (no auto-rebase before the gates).

- `CLAUDE.md` — "Pre-step: `vc-x1 sync` (still useful)" section
  rewritten around `--check`/`--no-check`. Output-shape bullets
  updated to describe the fatal check-mode error.

- `README.md` — sync usage examples + flag table + output-shape
  section updated. Push-stage table's `preflight` row updated to
  `vc-x1 sync --check`.

### 4. Stage-prefix log format: `push:STAGE:` → `push STAGE:`

Stage-tagged log lines were `push:preflight:`, `push:review:`,
… `push:finalize-claude:`. With nine prefixes plus subprocess
output, the colon-pair felt dense; a single space between "push"
and the stage parses faster.

- `src/push.rs` — nine stage prefixes renamed (preflight,
  review, message, commit-app, commit-claude, bookmark-both,
  push-app, finalize-claude, step). Bare `push: ...` lines
  (top-level, no stage) are unchanged.

  Side note: `push push-app:` now reads with the word "push"
  twice in a row. The stage's name is `push-app` because its
  job is to push the app repo; renaming it would touch the
  state-file format and `--from` values, which is wider surgery
  than warranted here.

### 5. Subprocess output hidden by default (visible with `-v`)

Subprocess stderr (jj's `Working copy now at ...`, `Rebased N
commits`, etc.) was emitted at `info!` in `common::run`, so it
showed by default. Combined with the dense `push:STAGE:` prefix
and the multi-stage flow, the output felt noisy. Drop subprocess
stderr to `debug!` — hidden by default, surfaced with `-v`. The
debug formatter already indents per-line two spaces, so when `-v`
is set, subprocess lines sit visually under the caller's `info!`
header. This change subsumes the originally-planned "indent
stderr two spaces in info!" fix.

- `src/common.rs` — `run` doc comment rewritten. Subprocess
  stderr success path: `info!("{stderr}")` → `debug!("{stderr}")`.
  Subprocess stdout: `debug!("  {stdout}")` → `debug!("{stdout}")`
  (drop the redundant inline indent — the formatter already
  indents debug lines).

  Scope note: `common::run` is shared by every subcommand, so this
  silences subprocess output globally (init, sync, finalize, clone,
  push). Trade-off accepted: high-volume jj chatter is rarely
  load-bearing for the user, and `-v` brings it back. If a specific
  subcommand needs a particular subprocess line surfaced, the right
  fix is an explicit `info!` in that subcommand at the moment the
  thing happens — not relying on subprocess passthrough.

### 6. Versioning + bookkeeping

- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — version `0.37.0` → `0.37.1`.
- `notes/todo.md` — Done entry added (covers all 5 fixes).

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress


## Todo

A markdown list of task to do in the near feature

 - Check for non-tracking-remote bookmarks during push and sync preflight as
   bookmarks that aren't trackng can't bre pushed.
 - Allow `vc-x1 push` to work on code or bot repo together or independantly and
   specifically we should be able to "squash" in th code repo just as we do in
   the bot repo.
 - Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
 - Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
 - Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
 - Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
 - Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
 - Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
 - Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
 - Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
 - Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

content.content:
---
vc-x1 0.37.1
---
vc-x1 0.37.1

Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes.

Repo set defaults to the dual-repo workspace pair (`.` and
`.claude`); override with `-R` / `--repo` for single-repo
projects or arbitrary multi-repo workspaces. Flag is repeatable
or comma-separated (e.g. `-R . -R .claude` or `-R .,.claude`).

Default is dry-run — re-run with --no-dry-run to apply. Per repo:
  - up-to-date        nothing to do
  - behind            fast-forward bookmark to remote
  - ahead             nothing to sync (local has unpushed work)
  - diverged          rebase local onto remote; fail on conflicts
  - no remote         bookmark has no @<remote> counterpart; skip

After the bookmark action, `@` is rebased onto the (possibly
advanced) bookmark if it isn't already a descendant, so trailing
working-copy writes (e.g. `.claude`'s `/exit` tail) don't end up
orphaned on a stale branch.

On any failure, every repo is reverted to its starting state via
`jj op restore`. Working-copy files are preserved across the
revert — the operation log rewinds but disk content stays.

vc-x1 0.37.1
---
vc-x1 0.37.1

Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes.

Repo set defaults to the dual-repo workspace pair (`.` and
`.claude`); override with `-R` / `--repo` for single-repo
projects or arbitrary multi-repo workspaces. Flag is repeatable
or comma-separated (e.g. `-R . -R .claude` or `-R .,.claude`).

Default is dry-run — re-run with --no-dry-run to apply. Per repo:
  - up-to-date        nothing to do
  - behind            fast-forward bookmark to remote
  - ahead             nothing to sync (local has unpushed work)
  - diverged          rebase local onto remote; fail on conflicts
  - no remote         bookmark has no @<remote> counterpart; skip

After the bookmark action, `@` is rebased onto the (possibly
advanced) bookmark if it isn't already a descendant, so trailing
working-copy writes (e.g. `.claude`'s `/exit` tail) don't end up
orphaned on a stale branch.

On any failure, every repo is reverted to its starting state via
`jj op restore`. Working-copy files are preserved across the
revert — the operation log rewinds but disk content stays.

/// Create Claude Code project symlink
    Symlink(symlink::SymlinkArgs),

    /// Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes
    #[command(long_about = "Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes.\n\n\
        Repo set defaults to the dual-repo workspace pair (`.` and\n\
        `.claude`); override with `-R` / `--repo` for single-repo\n\
        projects or arbitrary multi-repo workspaces. Flag is repeatable\n\
        or comma-separated (e.g. `-R . -R .claude` or `-R .,.claude`).\n\n\
        Default is dry-run — re-run with --no-dry-run to apply. Per repo:\n  \
          - up-to-date        nothing to do\n  \
          - behind            fast-forward bookmark to remote\n  \
          - ahead             nothing to sync (local has unpushed work)\n  \
          - diverged          rebase local onto remote; fail on conflicts\n  \
          - no remote         bookmark has no @<remote> counterpart; skip\n\n\
        After the bookmark action, `@` is rebased onto the (possibly\n\
        advanced) bookmark if it isn't already a descendant, so trailing\n\
        working-copy writes (e.g. `.claude`'s `/exit` tail) don't end up\n\
        orphaned on a stale branch.\n\n\
        On any failure, every repo is reverted to its starting state via\n\
        `jj op restore`. Working-copy files are preserved across the\n\
        revert — the operation log rewinds but disk content stays.\n\n\
        Output shape:\n  \
          - all-up-to-date: one-line summary (`sync: N repos, all up-to-date`)\n  \
          - action needed:  per-repo fetch + state + `dry-run` hint\n  \
          - --quiet:        no output; exit code signals success")]
    Sync(sync::SyncArgs),

    /// Squash, set bookmark, and/or push a jj repo
    #[command(long_about = "Squash, set bookmark, and/or push a jj repo.\n\n\
        Designed for the bot to atomically finalize its session repo:\n\
        --detach exits immediately, --delay waits for trailing writes,\n\
        --squash folds them in, --bookmark + --push sends it upstream.\n\
        Every flag is opt-in. See README.md for details.")]
    Finalize(finalize::FinalizeArgs),

content.text:
All changes integrated, 204 tests pass (gained 2 new sync parse tests), `vc-x1 --version` reports `0.37.1`.

**Smoke test result:** ran `vc-x1 push main` in your existing `tf-1` fixture — the fatal gitignore error fires exactly as designed:

```
error: push: state dir '.vc-x1' is not in /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/tf-1/work/.gitignore — add a '/.vc-x1' line so in-progress push-state files don't get committed
```

That fixture was created before the init/fixture auto-write change, so it's a real demo of the new contract: error is loud, error names the file and the line to add, no bypass flag tempts the user. Recreate the fixture (`vc-x1 test-fixture-rm ./tf-1 && vc-x1 test-fixture --path ./tf-1`) to get a clean `.gitignore` for further interactive testing.

**0.37.1 final scope:**

1. `src/push.rs` — editor template wording (explicit contract + auto-insertion notes); `parse_message` relaxed (first non-comment line = title, rest = body); two new parser tests.
2. `src/push.rs` + `src/init.rs` + `src/test_fixture.rs` — gitignore: fatal in `push`, auto-write `/.vc-x1` in init + fixture `GITIGNORE_CODE`; tests updated.
3. `src/sync.rs` + `src/push.rs` + `src/main.rs` — sync `--check`/`--no-check` rename; check mode fatal on action-needed; push preflight uses `--check`; `parse_check_flag` and `parse_check_no_check_conflict` tests added; `apply_args()` helper updated; long-about help text updated.
4. `src/push.rs` — 9 stage prefixes `push:STAGE:` → `push STAGE:`.
5. `src/common.rs` — subprocess stderr `info!` → `debug!`; stdout loses redundant inline indent. Hidden by default, `-v` surfaces.
6. `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — `0.37.0` → `0.37.1`.
7. `CLAUDE.md` + `README.md` — sync section rewritten around `--check`/`--no-check`; output-shape bullets describe the fatal mode; fixture-layout snippet adds `/.vc-x1`; push-stage table updated.
8. `notes/chores-05.md` — `0.37.1` entry expanded into six numbered subsections covering all fixes + rationale.
9. `notes/todo.md` — Done line text broadened, ref `[53]` anchor updated.

Pre-commit checklist green. Work complete. Please review. On approval I'll prep the commit title and body for a second approval before pushing.

content.text:
Mix-up of subcommand surfaces, not an install issue. `--check` / `--no-check` are on **`vc-x1 sync`**, not `vc-x1 push`:

```
vc-x1 sync -h
```

Push has its own `--dry-run` (preview the push stages without side effects) — different flag, different purpose. Push invokes sync internally with `--check` during preflight; you don't pass it through push.

So:
- `vc-x1 sync --help` → see `--check` / `--no-check`.
- `vc-x1 push --help` → push's own flags (including push's `--dry-run`, which is unchanged).
- `vc-x1 push main` → preflight runs `vc-x1 sync --check` for you; if anything's behind/diverged you get the fatal sync error before any approval gates appear.

Install is current (`vc-x1 0.37.1`). Want to try `vc-x1 sync -h` to confirm the new flags are visible?

wink@3900x 26-04-22T22:55:24.383Z:~/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/tf-1/work ((main))

content.text:
Good catch. Sync's "up-to-date" is bookmark-vs-remote scope — it intentionally ignores working-copy changes since for the push flow, having `@` non-empty is the whole point (that's what's about to be committed). But the wording reads as broader than it is.

Three ways to fix, increasing in scope:

**A) Wording-only clarification** — change `sync: 2 repos, all up-to-date` to `sync: 2 repos, all bookmarks up-to-date` (or `tracking up-to-date`). Honest about scope, no new logic. Fits 0.37.1.

**B) Add a working-copy summary line** — e.g. `sync: 2 repos, all up-to-date (./: 1 changed file, ./.claude: clean)`. Useful info but requires deciding the format and adding a `jj st`-equivalent probe per repo. Probably 0.37.2.

**C) Treat working-copy changes as "action needed" in `--check`** — fatal if `@` is non-empty. Forces discipline but conflates two concepts (sync vs commit) and would break the push preflight (which deliberately runs sync before its own commit stages). Probably wrong.

My recommendation:

- **(A) for 0.37.1** — quick, cohesive with "scope clarity" theme of the polish pass.
- **(B) as 0.37.2** — get a fresh design conversation about format (per-repo stat? overall count? compactness?), since it touches user-facing output shape.

Sound right? If yes, I'll make the (A) edit and roll it into the same 0.37.1 commit. (B) gets a follow-up todo entry.
