# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress


## Todo

A markdown list of task to do in the near feature

 - Check for non-tracking-remote bookmarks during push and sync preflight as
   bookmarks that aren't trackng can't bre pushed.
 - Allow `vc-x1 push` to work on code or bot repo together or independantly and
   specifically we should be able to "squash" in th code repo just as we do in
   the bot repo.
 - Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
 - Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
 - Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
 - Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
 - Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
 - Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
 - Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
 - Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
 - Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]
- push polish: --dry-run, --step, non-tty detection, gitignore warning (0.37.0-5) [48]
- push docs + workflow migration — CLAUDE.md rewrite + README section (0.37.0) [48]
- First-dogfood polish for push: editor template, gitignore-fatal, sync --check, log prefix, quieter subprocess (0.37.1) [53]

# References

[4]: /notes/chores-01.md#refactor-and-add-desc-subcommand
[5]: /notes/chores-01.md#claude-repo-issue-070-dev0-through-dev2

### 6. Versioning + bookkeeping

- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — version `0.37.0` → `0.37.1`.
- `notes/todo.md` — Done entry added (covers all 5 fixes).
input.new_string:
### 6. Sync up-to-date wording: clarify scope (bookmarks, not work tree)

`sync` reported `sync: N repos, all up-to-date` regardless of whether
the working copy had uncommitted changes. Reads as broader than its
actual scope (bookmark-vs-remote tracking only). User dogfood: ran
`vc-x1 sync` after `echo hello > hello.txt`, got "all up-to-date"
while `jj st` showed the new file — confusing.

Wording-only fix here. The richer "tell me about working-copy state
too" feature is a follow-up — see open-question subsection below.

- `src/sync.rs` — phase-2 summary `sync: {n} {noun}, all up-to-date`
  → `sync: {n} {noun}, all bookmarks up-to-date`. The per-repo
  `State::UpToDate` line (`{repo}: up-to-date`) is unchanged — its
  scope is already clear from context.
- `src/main.rs` — sync `long_about` output-shape example updated.
- `CLAUDE.md` — output-shape clean bullet adds a scope note: "scope
  is bookmark-vs-remote tracking, not working-copy cleanliness".
- `README.md` — same scope note in the output-shape section, plus
  a `jj st` pointer for the working-copy case.

### 7. Versioning + bookkeeping

- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — version `0.37.0` → `0.37.1`.
- `notes/todo.md` — Done entry added (covers all six fixes); new
  Todo entry added for the deferred working-copy-signal work.

## Open: sync up-to-date should mention working-copy state

Deferred from 0.37.1's wording fix (subsection 6 above). Wording
clarified the scope (`all bookmarks up-to-date`) but the user's
underlying need was a single command that says "you have N pending
files in repo X" without having to run `jj st` per repo.

Design-open. Sketch of the question space:

- **Format.** Per-repo line under the summary
  (`./: 1 pending file (hello.txt)`) vs. compact aggregate
  (`sync: 2 repos clean, 1 with pending changes`)?
- **Detail level.** Just count? Count + names (cap at N)? Stat-style?
- **Always-on vs --status.** Show every run, or gate behind a
  `--working-copy` / `--all` flag so the cheap clean-case stays
  one line?
- **Scope under push preflight.** Push always commits working-copy
  changes in the next stage, so a "you have pending changes" note
  during preflight is noise. Maybe sync grows the signal but
  `vc-x1 sync --check` (the form push uses) suppresses it?

Probable shape: per-repo "N pending file(s)" line under the summary
when non-zero, no flag needed, suppressed by `--quiet`. But the
push-preflight noise question needs a deliberate answer first.

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress


## Todo

A markdown list of task to do in the near feature

 - sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary (per-repo
   pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only fix shipped in
   0.37.1; this is the design+impl of the actual signal. [54]
 - Check for non-tracking-remote bookmarks during push and sync preflight as
   bookmarks that aren't trackng can't bre pushed.
 - Allow `vc-x1 push` to work on code or bot repo together or independantly and
   specifically we should be able to "squash" in th code repo just as we do in
   the bot repo.
 - Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
 - Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
 - Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
 - Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
 - Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
 - Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
 - Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
 - Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
 - Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]
- push polish: --dry-run, --step, non-tty detection, gitignore warning (0.37.0-5) [48]
- push docs + workflow migration — CLAUDE.md rewrite + README section (0.37.0) [48]
- First-dogfood polish for push: editor template, gitignore-fatal, sync --check, log prefix, quieter subprocess (0.37.1) [53]

# References

input.new_string:
- Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both repos in
   one shot. Natural home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
 - Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards through to
   the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation). 0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`;
   `--no-check` would be the user-opt-in to "auto-rebase under the gates,
   I trust the sync state". Default stays `--check`.

content.text: Added to todo. My read of "for completeness": push grows `--check`/`--no-check` that pass through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` call. Default stays `--check` (the safe behavior we just shipped); `vc-x1 push main --no-check` would be the explicit opt-in to "auto-rebase if behind/diverged, I accept that under the gates." Captured that framing in the entry — feel free to redirect when you pick it up.

content.text:
Proposed commit message:

**Title** (49 chars):

```
feat: first-dogfood polish for push (0.37.1)
```

**Body**:

```
First dogfood of vc-x1 push surfaced seven papercuts in the
same session — small, all "post-ship polish", shipped together
as a single-step 0.37.1. Full detail in
notes/chores-05.md > First-dogfood polish for push (0.37.1).

- src/push.rs: editor template states the contract;
  parse_message relaxed to first-line/rest (no required blank line)
- src/push.rs + src/init.rs + src/test_fixture.rs: gitignore
  coherence fatal in push; init + fixture auto-write /.vc-x1
- src/sync.rs + src/push.rs + src/main.rs: --no-dry-run renamed
  to --no-check / --check; check mode fatal on action-needed;
  push preflight uses --check
- src/push.rs: nine stage prefixes push:STAGE: → push STAGE:
- src/common.rs: subprocess stderr drops to debug! (hidden by
  default, surfaced with -v)
- src/sync.rs: up-to-date summary clarifies scope
  ("all bookmarks up-to-date")
- Cargo.toml / Cargo.lock: 0.37.0 → 0.37.1
- CLAUDE.md / README.md: sync section, output-shape, fixture
  layout, push-stage table updated
- notes/chores-05.md: 0.37.1 entry (seven subsections + open
  follow-up sketch for the working-copy signal)
- notes/todo.md: Done line + three new Todo entries (working-copy
  signal [54], status subcommand, --check on push)
```

OK to run `vc-x1 push main` with this title/body?

lastPrompt: sg, we're ready to commit this sync change then

content.text:
Push went through commit-app → commit-claude → bookmark-both → push-app successfully (the **app remote has the commit**). It failed at `finalize-claude` because `.claude`'s `main` bookmark isn't tracking its remote:

```
error: bookmark 'main' has non-tracking remote 'main@origin' —
run `jj bookmark track main --remote=origin -R .../.claude` to fix
```

This is exactly the case the existing top-of-todo entry calls out ("Check for non-tracking-remote bookmarks during push and sync preflight…") — dogfood just hit it.

Current state:
- App repo: commit landed, bookmark advanced, **pushed to remote** ✓
- `.claude` repo: commit landed, bookmark advanced locally, **not pushed**
- Saved push state likely still present (didn't reach completion)

Suggested fix per the error message:

```
jj bookmark track main --remote=origin -R /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/.claude
vc-x1 finalize --repo /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/.claude --squash --push main --delay 10 --detach --log /tmp/vc-x1-finalize.log
```

(That's the "Manual finalize fallback" recipe from CLAUDE.md, with the bookmark-track step prepended.)

Want me to:
1. Run the two commands above to recover, or
2. Pause so you can inspect first (`vc-x1 push --status`, `jj log -R .claude`, etc.)?

content.text:
Good catch — there's a separate, bigger problem you spotted. Two distinct issues:

**1. Your real catch: `tf-1/` got committed and pushed to the app remote.**

The app repo's `.gitignore` (above) doesn't list `/tf-1`. jj snapshotted the working copy and folded all 56 fixture files (bare-git remote dirs, hooks, objects, refs) into the commit. The commit landed on the app remote that way.

**2. `.claude` bookmark not tracking — pre-existing, unrelated to tf-1.**

`jj log -R .claude` shows `main` and `main@origin` as separate refs (not a tracked pair). The session repo has been like this since setup; just first time it bit us because it's the first push attempt on `.claude`. Independent from #1.

**Recovery is two halves:**

- **`.claude` side** — track the bookmark, then re-run finalize to push. Conservative.
- **App side** — the tf-1 commit is on the remote. Options:
  - (a) Force-push a fixup that strips `tf-1/*` (destructive, needs your OK).
  - (b) Add `/tf-1` to `.gitignore`, commit a follow-up that removes the tracked tf-1 files (non-destructive, leaves history with the noise commit but cleans current state).
  - (c) Leave tf-1 in history; just add `/tf-1` to `.gitignore` going forward (cheapest, history stays cluttered).

Probably want **(b)** — clean current state without rewriting published history. Force-push (a) is safer in this small project but breaks the "no destructive without explicit ask" default.

Want me to inspect first (`vc-x1 push --status`, etc.) before doing anything? Or pick a recovery path?

Good, straightforward per the CLAUDE.md "Late changes after push" pattern. Here's what I propose to run:

```bash
# 1. delete the fixture from working copy
rm -rf /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/tf-1

# 2. add /tf-* to .gitignore so future fixtures don't re-trigger this
#    (using the glob since the test-fixture default base name is
#    vc-x1-test-<timestamp>, but your manual naming was tf-1 — glob
#    catches both patterns)
```

Actually hold on — your fixture path was explicit `./tf-1`, while `vc-x1 test-fixture` defaults to `$TMPDIR/vc-x1-test-<ts>`. The explicit in-repo path is the unusual case. Two questions before I touch `.gitignore`:

1. **Should `.gitignore` get `/tf-1` (specific) or `/tf-*` (glob)?** I lean `/tf-*` since you'd probably use `./tf-2`, `./tf-3` next time by habit, but specific is fine if you'd rather keep it minimal.

2. **Should `vc-x1 test-fixture` refuse to scaffold inside the repo?** Separate todo-worthy question — the ergonomic fix is at the tool level, not just `.gitignore`. If `--path ./tf-1` resolved inside the workspace and test-fixture warned/errored, none of us would be in this mess today.

Once `.gitignore` is sorted, the rest is the documented recovery:

```bash
# 3. squash the removal into the just-pushed feat commit
jj squash --ignore-immutable -R /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1

# 4. bump main to the squashed commit
jj bookmark set main -r @- -R /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1

# 5. force-push (non-fast-forward; jj will warn/require confirmation)
jj git push --bookmark main -R /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1
```

Confirm the `.gitignore` pattern + OK to run all five, and I'll do it.

Right — you're correct. `jj squash` folded `@` into `@-`, which is the commit `main` already pointed at, so `main` just came along (the `main*` in your prompt confirms it's ahead of remote and ready to push).

The `jj bookmark set main -r @- -R .` step in CLAUDE.md's "Late changes after push" recipe is over-cautious for this case — it'd only be needed if the squash landed somewhere other than where `main` already was. Worth a small doc polish (todo: tighten the recipe).

Next step is just the force-push:

```bash
jj git push --bookmark main -R /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1
```

If it refuses on non-fast-forward, jj usually hints the exact `--allow-*` flag to add. Go ahead.

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress


## Todo

A markdown list of task to do in the near feature

 - sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary (per-repo
   pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only fix shipped in
   0.37.1; this is the design+impl of the actual signal. [54]
 - Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both repos in
   one shot. Natural home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
 - Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards through to
   the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation). 0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`;
   `--no-check` would be the user-opt-in to "auto-rebase under the gates,
   I trust the sync state". Default stays `--check`.
 - Check for non-tracking-remote bookmarks during push and sync preflight as
   bookmarks that aren't trackng can't bre pushed.
 - Allow `vc-x1 push` to work on code or bot repo together or independantly and
   specifically we should be able to "squash" in th code repo just as we do in
   the bot repo.

 - Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric

input.description: Find bookmark setup in init + test_fixture

content.text:
Diagnosis from op log + code inspection:

**Finding:** this `.claude` was bootstrapped via `jj git init --colocate` + `jj git fetch` + manual `jj bookmark set main -r main@origin` (see op log entries from 3 hours ago and 23 hours ago). That sequence positions the bookmark but **doesn't establish tracking** with the remote — tracking in jj is a separate explicit operation.

Compare:
- `src/init.rs:482-485` DOES call `jj bookmark track main --remote=origin` after Step 10's re-init. So `vc-x1 init`-created repos get tracking.
- `src/test_fixture.rs` doesn't explicitly track — but test fixtures do the **initial push from scratch** which auto-establishes tracking as a side effect.
- Your `.claude` didn't go through either path cleanly — either predates the current init flow, or was hand-bootstrapped at some point.

So the root-cause story is: **this particular `.claude` missed the tracking step at setup.** One-time fix.

Plus a small bonus observation: your `.claude` has an `autocomplete@origin: (divergent)` from back in 0.26.0 days — unrelated to this issue but worth a future tidy-up.

Ready to run the recovery. Green light for:

```bash
jj bookmark track main --remote=origin -R /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/.claude
vc-x1 finalize --repo /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/.claude --squash --push main --delay 10 --detach --log /tmp/vc-x1-finalize.log
```

content.text:
Clarifying finding: `.claude` on **fwlaptop** pushes fine — the op log shows multiple successful `jj git push --bookmark main -R .../.claude` operations (3 hours ago, 23 hours ago, 1 day ago). So tracking IS established on fwlaptop.

Refined diagnosis: **tracking is per-workspace, stored in the local jj store, not in shared git refs.** When you sync between machines, git refs transfer, but jj's per-workspace tracking metadata doesn't. So:

- fwlaptop's `.claude` workspace: tracking established at some earlier setup → pushes work.
- 3900x's `.claude` workspace: tracking never established here → pushes refuse.
- Every `vc-x1 sync` between them fetches refs and updates `main@origin`, but doesn't backfill the tracking flag.

That reshapes the investigation:

1. **This specific incident:** run `jj bookmark track main --remote=origin -R .../.claude` once on 3900x. Per-workspace one-time fix.
2. **Systemic fix (the existing todo entry):** `vc-x1 sync` (or push preflight) should detect and warn about (or repair?) missing tracking — exactly the case this dogfood just documented. Worth updating the todo entry's body with this diagnosis so you've got the "why it happens" written down.

Want me to:
- (a) Run the recovery (track + finalize-push on 3900x), and
- (b) Update the todo entry with the per-workspace-tracking finding?

toolUseResult.newString:
- Check for non-tracking-remote bookmarks during push and sync preflight as
   bookmarks that aren't tracking can't be pushed. Diagnosed 2026-04-22
   dogfood: jj's tracking state is **per-workspace** (local `.jj` store),
   not shared via git refs. Sync across machines transfers refs but not
   the tracking flag — so a fresh workspace fetched-into never auto-tracks.
   Recovery: `jj bookmark track <b> --remote=<r> -R <repo>`. Fix shape:
   sync/push preflight should detect missing tracking per (workspace,
   bookmark, remote) and either (a) refuse with the exact remediation
   command, or (b) self-heal by calling `jj bookmark track` when the
   remote ref already exists. (a) is safer; (b) is friendlier.
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress


## Todo

A markdown list of task to do in the near feature

 - sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary (per-repo
   pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only fix shipped in
   0.37.1; this is the design+impl of the actual signal. [54]
 - Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both repos in
   one shot. Natural home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
 - Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards through to
   the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation). 0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`;
   `--no-check` would be the user-opt-in to "auto-rebase under the gates,
   I trust the sync state". Default stays `--check`.
 - `vc-x1 test-fixture` should refuse `--path` values that resolve inside
   the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood surfaced this:
   `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj snapshot the fixture's bare-git
   remotes into the commit — a 56-file noise blob that got force-push'd
   off the remote later. Tool-level prevention, not `.gitignore`.
 - Tighten CLAUDE.md "Late changes after push" recipe: drop the
   `jj bookmark set <bookmark> -r @- -R .` line when the squash target
   is `@-` (bookmark follows naturally). Keep it only for the cases
   where squash lands somewhere the bookmark isn't already.
 - Check for non-tracking-remote bookmarks during push and sync preflight as
   bookmarks that aren't trackng can't bre pushed.
 - Allow `vc-x1 push` to work on code or bot repo together or independantly and
   specifically we should be able to "squash" in th code repo just as we do in
   the bot repo.
 - Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
 - Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
 - Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
 - Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
 - Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
 - Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
 - Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
 - Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
 - Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]

message.content:
An interesting tid bit, the file timestamp displayed by gitk (I use it to quickly look at the commit history) is near the "Birth" date as reported by stat:
wink@3900x 26-04-23T01:12:12.024Z:~/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/.claude ((main))
$ stat c8b17196-67ee-4509-b278-67e45fc8d7e4.jsonl
  File: c8b17196-67ee-4509-b278-67e45fc8d7e4.jsonl
  Size: 1348039       Blocks: 2648       IO Block: 4096   regular file
Device: 9,0    Inode: 196643464   Links: 1
Access: (0600/-rw-------)  Uid: ( 1000/    wink)   Gid: (  985/   users)
Access: 2026-04-22 18:11:47.080479582 -0700
Modify: 2026-04-22 18:11:47.080699353 -0700
Change: 2026-04-22 18:11:47.080699353 -0700
 Birth: 2026-04-22 14:17:44.356988608 -0700
wink@3900x 26-04-23T01:14:32.495Z:~/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/.claude ((main))

but the time shown by gitk is:
2026-04-22-14:18:06

That's weird

content.text:
Reasonable explanation: gitk by default shows **author date**, not committer date, and jj preserves author dates across squashes.

- File Birth `14:17:44` → jsonl first created when the session started.
- gitk `14:18:06` → author date, stamped when the commit was first *described* (~22s after the jsonl started being written). Stable across every subsequent squash.
- File Modify `18:11:47` → most recent write to the jsonl (ongoing session traffic).
- `jj log` would show **committer** date (~16:08 earlier today for the chores commit; closer to "now" after the finalize squash).

So there's no lost history — the two timestamps just encode different things:

- **Author date**: "when the work was first articulated." jj keeps this pinned to the original commit description, so it ages with the feature's conceptual origin, not with the latest rewrite.
- **Committer date**: "when this specific commit object came into being." Moves forward on every rebase/squash.

`gitk --date=relative` or `gitk --date=iso` + changing the "show author/committer" setting would swap which one displays. Most GUI tools default to author date because it's the more "stable" timestamp in a rebase-heavy workflow. Nothing's wrong here, just jj + git metadata doing their thing.

message.content: Where is "Author" date I don't see that in stat's dates?

content.text:
My bad, sloppy wording. Author date isn't in `stat` at all — it's stored **inside the git commit object**, not on the filesystem.

A commit object looks like:

```
tree <sha>
parent <sha>
author Wink Saville <wink@saville.com> 1745370486 -0700
committer Wink Saville <wink@saville.com> 1745380694 -0700

<commit message>
```

Those two unix timestamps are recorded by git when the commit is created and live inside the object in `.git/objects/`. gitk reads them from there.

`stat`'s four dates are purely filesystem:

- **Access**: last read
- **Modify**: last write to the file's *bytes*
- **Change**: last write to the *inode* (permissions, size, content)
- **Birth**: inode creation time

Zero overlap with git's author/committer dates — they're two independent recording systems that happen to be near each other in your case because the sequence was: `touch jsonl (Birth 14:17:44)` → `describe commit (author date ~14:18:06)` → …much later… `file still being written (Modify 18:11:47)`.

To see the author date directly:

```bash
git log -1 --format='%ai  author%n%ci  committer' main -R /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/.claude
# or with jj:
jj log -r main -R /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/.claude -T 'author.timestamp().ago() ++ " (author)\n" ++ committer.timestamp().ago() ++ " (committer)"'
```

$ git log -1 --format='%ai  author%n%ci  committer' main -R /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/.claude
2026-04-22 14:18:06 -0700  author
2026-04-22 18:04:59 -0700  committer
wink@3900x 26-04-23T01:20:46.146Z:~/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/.claude ((main))

content.text: Nice — matches the story: author `14:18:06` = original describe, committer `18:04:59` = ~10s after finalize launched (its delay window expiring and squash happening). Everything lines up.

message.content: That explained, does it explain the non-tracking bookmark?

content.text:
No — completely independent systems. Different storage, different replication behavior.

| | Storage | Travels with... |
|---|---|---|
| Author/committer date | Inside the git commit object (`.git/objects/`) | Every clone/fetch/push — content-addressed, immutable |
| Bookmark tracking flag | jj's per-workspace store (`.jj/repo/store/...`) | Nothing. Local to one workspace. |

The author/committer-date discovery was about **commit metadata** (stable, synced everywhere). The non-tracking issue is about **workspace metadata** (mutable, never synced). They don't touch.

Analogy: your chat app syncs messages (the commit object) but not your local "read/unread" flags (the tracking state). Moving to a new device gives you all the messages but resets the read/unread.

So:

- Commits on 3900x and fwlaptop: identical, byte-for-byte. ✓ (author/committer dates included)
- Bookmark tracking on 3900x: not set up here.
- Bookmark tracking on fwlaptop: was set up there at some point.
- `vc-x1 sync` transferring data between them: moves refs and objects, doesn't touch the tracking flag.

The earlier diagnosis stands — fix is still the per-workspace one-time `jj bookmark track` (which we just did on 3900x).

lastPrompt: That explained, does it explain the non-tracking bookmark?

message.content: So is sync detecting non-tracking during preflight now?

content.text:
No — still open. The todo entry I updated earlier captures it:

> Check for non-tracking-remote bookmarks during push and sync preflight…

Current sync's `classify()` only compares local-bookmark position to remote-ref position (→ `Behind` / `Ahead` / `Diverged` / `UpToDate`). It doesn't inspect the tracking flag. A non-tracking bookmark where local==remote would read as `UpToDate`; local-ahead-of-remote (this session's case) reads as `Ahead` (benign, not fatal under `--check`). Either way, the non-tracking condition flies under the radar until `jj git push` actually tries and refuses.

Implementing the detection is small (jj exposes tracking status via `jj bookmark list --tracked` or the template language `remote_bookmark.tracked()`), but the **policy** decision is the interesting part — refuse with a pointer to `jj bookmark track …`, or self-heal by calling track automatically when the remote ref already exists? The todo entry sketches both options; picking one is the next step when you work that item.

message.content: ok, so generally, all commands which can create|modify a repo we need to check for --tracked bookmarks and error loudly. And, of course, init needs to setup tracking which I pretty sure it does already

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress


## Todo

A markdown list of task to do in the near feature

 - sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary (per-repo
   pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only fix shipped in
   0.37.1; this is the design+impl of the actual signal. [54]
 - Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both repos in
   one shot. Natural home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
 - Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards through to
   the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation). 0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`;
   `--no-check` would be the user-opt-in to "auto-rebase under the gates,
   I trust the sync state". Default stays `--check`.
 - `vc-x1 test-fixture` should refuse `--path` values that resolve inside
   the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood surfaced this:
   `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj snapshot the fixture's bare-git
   remotes into the commit — a 56-file noise blob that got force-push'd
   off the remote later. Tool-level prevention, not `.gitignore`.
 - Tighten CLAUDE.md "Late changes after push" recipe: drop the
   `jj bookmark set <bookmark> -r @- -R .` line when the squash target
   is `@-` (bookmark follows naturally). Keep it only for the cases
   where squash lands somewhere the bookmark isn't already.
 - Check for non-tracking-remote bookmarks during push and sync preflight as
   bookmarks that aren't tracking can't be pushed. Diagnosed 2026-04-22
   dogfood: jj's tracking state is **per-workspace** (local `.jj` store),
   not shared via git refs. Sync across machines transfers refs but not
   the tracking flag — so a fresh workspace fetched-into never auto-tracks.
   Recovery: `jj bookmark track <b> --remote=<r> -R <repo>`. Fix shape:
   sync/push preflight should detect missing tracking per (workspace,
   bookmark, remote) and either (a) refuse with the exact remediation
   command, or (b) self-heal by calling `jj bookmark track` when the
   remote ref already exists. (a) is safer; (b) is friendlier.
 - Allow `vc-x1 push` to work on code or bot repo together or independantly and
   specifically we should be able to "squash" in th code repo just as we do in
   the bot repo.
 - Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
 - Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
 - Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
 - Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
 - Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
 - Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
 - Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
 - Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
 - Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]
- push polish: --dry-run, --step, non-tty detection, gitignore warning (0.37.0-5) [48]
- push docs + workflow migration — CLAUDE.md rewrite + README section (0.37.0) [48]

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress


## Todo

A markdown list of task to do in the near feature

 - sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary (per-repo
   pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only fix shipped in
   0.37.1; this is the design+impl of the actual signal. [54]
 - Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both repos in
   one shot. Natural home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
 - Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards through to
   the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation). 0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`;
   `--no-check` would be the user-opt-in to "auto-rebase under the gates,
   I trust the sync state". Default stays `--check`.
 - `vc-x1 test-fixture` should refuse `--path` values that resolve inside
   the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood surfaced this:
   `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj snapshot the fixture's bare-git
   remotes into the commit — a 56-file noise blob that got force-push'd
   off the remote later. Tool-level prevention, not `.gitignore`.
 - Tighten CLAUDE.md "Late changes after push" recipe: drop the
   `jj bookmark set <bookmark> -r @- -R .` line when the squash target
   is `@-` (bookmark follows naturally). Keep it only for the cases
   where squash lands somewhere the bookmark isn't already.
 - Non-tracking-remote bookmark detection across every repo-modifying
   command. Diagnosed 2026-04-22 dogfood: jj's tracking state is
   **per-workspace** (local `.jj` store), not shared via git refs.
   Sync across machines transfers refs but not the tracking flag —
   so a fresh workspace fetched-into never auto-tracks. The failure
   surfaces only when `jj git push` is attempted, which is too late
   (push-app already succeeded in our case).

   **Policy (decided):** error loudly, with the exact
   `jj bookmark track <b> --remote=<r> -R <repo>` remediation
   command. No self-heal — keeps the fix explicit and visible.

   **Scope (every command that creates or mutates repo state):**
   - `vc-x1 init`: already correct (Step 10 calls `jj bookmark track`).
     Add the check as a post-condition sanity assertion anyway.
   - `vc-x1 clone`: does `git clone` then `jj git init --colocate`.
     Whether that combination auto-establishes tracking in jj's
     workspace store is unclear; probably needs an explicit
     `jj bookmark track` for each cloned bookmark after the init.
     Verify and fix.
   - `vc-x1 test-fixture`: currently relies on first-push to
     auto-track. Robust enough for tests but tighten with explicit
     track for symmetry with init.
   - `vc-x1 sync` preflight: detect + error.
   - `vc-x1 push` preflight: detect + error (before any mutation).
   - `vc-x1 finalize`: detect + error before the squash, so a
     failed push doesn't leave a half-finalized state.

   Shared helper: `common::verify_tracking(repo, bookmark, remote)
   -> Result<(), Err>` or similar. Probably use
   `jj bookmark list --tracked -T <template>` under the hood
   rather than parsing human-readable output.
 - Allow `vc-x1 push` to work on code or bot repo together or independantly and
   specifically we should be able to "squash" in th code repo just as we do in
   the bot repo.
 - Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
 - Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
 - Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
 - Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
 - Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
 - Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
 - Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
 - Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
 - Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]
- push polish: --dry-run, --step, non-tty detection, gitignore warning (0.37.0-5) [48]
- push docs + workflow migration — CLAUDE.md rewrite + README section (0.37.0) [48]
- First-dogfood polish for push: editor template, gitignore-fatal, sync --check, log prefix, quieter subprocess (0.37.1) [53]

message.content: I'm also thinking when every command exits we output if main is tracking or not on the "current repo", thoughts?

content.text:
Honest pushback worth hearing before you commit to it:

**Concern 1 — fights the 0.37.1 quieter-output work.** We just moved subprocess stderr to `debug!` specifically to reduce terminal noise. A per-command always-on tracking line partially undoes that. Commands like `vc-x1 list` or `vc-x1 chid` that are pure reads would grow a trailing bookkeeping line on every invocation.

**Concern 2 — redundant with detect-and-error.** The other todo (the one we just expanded) catches the broken case at the moment it matters: sync/push/finalize refuse with a loud error + exact remediation. If that lands, a passive "tracking: yes" line on every command is low signal — you're watching for "no," and "no" already errors loudly.

**Concern 3 — "current repo" is underdefined in a dual-repo workspace.** What does `vc-x1 chid` print when cwd = project root? Just the app? Both? What if someone runs from `~/` with no repo?

**Three refinements, best-to-worst in my read:**

1. **Silent when correct, loud when broken, on exit of repo-mutating commands.** Catches drift without taxing clean runs. Combined with the preflight detect-and-error, it's belt-and-suspenders on the same signal.

2. **Part of the `vc-x1 status` subcommand** (already in todo as `status|st`). Users who want the snapshot run it; users who don't aren't taxed. Natural home alongside working-copy state + diverged bookmarks.

3. **The always-on version you proposed.** Works, but costs one line on every invocation forever.

My lean: (2) is probably where this belongs long-term — a single place users go when they want "how is my workspace?" — with (1) as a cheap lightweight catch for mutation commands.

Does that reframe it, or do you want the always-on version anyway?

message.content: I was suggesting that just while we're looking for the culprit and could add it on entry as well as exit then we absolutely know "which command" is causing it if entry and exit are different, if it something not related to vc-x1 will notify us of that with the "entry" (again temporary).

lastPrompt: I was suggesting that just while we're looking for the culprit and could add it on entry as well as exit then we absolutely know "which command" is causing it if entry and exit are different, if it so…

content.text:
Ah, that reframes it completely — that's a diagnostic probe, not a permanent feature. Makes total sense, and it's a clean technique:

| Entry | Exit | Culprit |
|---|---|---|
| tracked | tracked | Not this command. |
| tracked | NOT tracked | **This command broke it.** |
| NOT tracked | (any) | Something between the previous command and this one. |
| NOT tracked | tracked | Rare, but interesting — something's repairing it. |

So the probe localizes blame to either "this vc-x1 command" or "something external happened between invocations." Temporary scaffolding, greppable, rip it out once the culprit is caught.

Concrete shape:

```
track-probe enter vc-x1 sync: app(main)=tracked, .claude(main)=tracked
track-probe exit  vc-x1 sync: app(main)=tracked, .claude(main)=NOT_TRACKED  ← aha
```

Where:
- **Where**: `main.rs` command dispatcher, before + after `run_command(...)`.
- **What**: tracking status of `main` in both repos of the dual-repo workspace (app at `.`, session at `.claude`). Probes both unconditionally since that's our focus.
- **How**: `jj bookmark list --tracked main -T '<template>' -R <repo>` — machine-readable, no human-output parsing.
- **Output level**: `info!` with a `track-probe:` prefix, so it's visible by default (the whole point is catching the culprit) and easy to `grep -v track-probe` later, AND easy to find/remove when retiring.
- **Graceful degradation**: if the repo doesn't exist, or jj errors, or the bookmark doesn't exist, print `track-probe enter X: unavailable (<reason>)` and continue — don't block the command under investigation.

Scope is small — maybe 30 lines in `main.rs` + a helper. No flag, no config, temporary. Separate commit labeled something like `chore: temp tracking probe on entry/exit (investigation)` with a clear note in the commit body that it'll be reverted once diagnosed.

Green light to implement and commit as a standalone follow-up to 0.37.1?

message.content: y

46	/// on every subcommand (transitively). Top-level skips `before_help`
47	/// because its own `about` already carries the name+version+tagline.
48	/// Walks via `mut_subcommand` so individual subcommand
49	/// `#[command(long_about = ...)]` blocks don't have to repeat the
50	/// banner text.
51	fn cli_with_banner() -> clap::Command {
52	    fn add_to_subs(mut cmd: clap::Command) -> clap::Command {
53	        let names: Vec<String> = cmd
54	            .get_subcommands()
55	            .map(|c| c.get_name().to_string())
56	            .collect();
57	        for name in names {
58	            cmd = cmd.mut_subcommand(name, add_with_banner);
59	        }
60	        cmd
61	    }
62	    fn add_with_banner(mut cmd: clap::Command) -> clap::Command {
63	        cmd = cmd.before_help(BANNER);

66	            .map(|c| c.get_name().to_string())
67	            .collect();

70	        }
71	        cmd
72	    }
73	    add_to_subs(Cli::command())
74	}

when non-zero, no flag needed, suppressed by `--quiet`. But the
push-preflight noise question needs a deliberate answer first.

## Temporary bookmark-tracking diagnostic probe (0.37.2)

0.37.1's first-dogfood push hit a `bookmark 'main' has non-tracking
remote 'main@origin'` error in the `.claude` repo during the
`finalize-claude` stage. Recovery was one-time: `jj bookmark track
main --remote=origin -R .claude`. But the open question is *when*
and *why* the tracking flag got lost — the fwlaptop op log shows
`.claude` pushing fine from there, so the 3900x workspace ended
up in a different state at some historical point.

The detect-and-error work (`notes/todo.md` top entry) is the
long-term policy fix. This chore is a **temporary diagnostic**
to localize the culprit: probe `main`-bookmark tracking status on
entry and exit of every vc-x1 command. If entry and exit differ
during a single command, that command is the culprit; if entry
differs from the previous command's exit, something *between*
invocations broke it (external tooling, manual jj commands, etc.).

Output shape:

```
track-probe enter vc-x1 push: app(main)=tracked, .claude(main)=tracked
track-probe exit  vc-x1 push: app(main)=tracked, .claude(main)=NOT_TRACKED
```

- `src/main.rs` — `use std::path::Path;` added. Two new functions:
  `track_probe(phase, command_name)` prints the status line for
  both repos; `track_probe_one(repo, bookmark, remote)` queries
  jj via `jj bookmark list --tracked <bookmark> -R <repo>`. The
  tracked-list entry includes a line starting with `@<remote>:`
  when the bookmark is tracking that remote; absence means
  not-tracking.
- `src/main.rs` — dispatch block wraps the `match cli.command`
  in a `let exit_code = match ...;` binding, with `track_probe`
  calls immediately before and after. Skipped when `is_detached_exec`
  (the `finalize --exec` re-entry) since probe noise in that log
  isn't useful.
- Graceful degradation — if the repo doesn't exist, missing-`.jj/`
  detection emits `no-jj`; subprocess failure emits `err(<first line>)`.
  Never blocks the wrapped command.
- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — `0.37.1` → `0.37.2`.

**How to rip it out** once the culprit is identified:

```bash
grep -rn 'track-probe\|track_probe' src/
```

Three places to touch:
1. `src/main.rs` — delete the two functions (`track_probe`,
   `track_probe_one`) and the `use std::path::Path` import if
   nothing else needs it.
2. `src/main.rs` — revert the dispatch to plain
   `match cli.command { ... }` (drop the `let exit_code = ...`
   binding, the two `track_probe(...)` calls, and the
   `command_name` binding).
3. `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — bump to the next appropriate
   version.

Net removal: ~80 lines.

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress


## Todo

A markdown list of task to do in the near feature

 - sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary (per-repo
   pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only fix shipped in
   0.37.1; this is the design+impl of the actual signal. [54]
 - Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both repos in
   one shot. Natural home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
 - Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards through to
   the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation). 0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`;
   `--no-check` would be the user-opt-in to "auto-rebase under the gates,
   I trust the sync state". Default stays `--check`.
 - `vc-x1 test-fixture` should refuse `--path` values that resolve inside
   the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood surfaced this:
   `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj snapshot the fixture's bare-git
   remotes into the commit — a 56-file noise blob that got force-push'd
   off the remote later. Tool-level prevention, not `.gitignore`.
 - Tighten CLAUDE.md "Late changes after push" recipe: drop the
   `jj bookmark set <bookmark> -r @- -R .` line when the squash target
   is `@-` (bookmark follows naturally). Keep it only for the cases
   where squash lands somewhere the bookmark isn't already.
 - Non-tracking-remote bookmark detection across every repo-modifying
   command. Diagnosed 2026-04-22 dogfood: jj's tracking state is
   **per-workspace** (local `.jj` store), not shared via git refs.
   Sync across machines transfers refs but not the tracking flag —
   so a fresh workspace fetched-into never auto-tracks. The failure
   surfaces only when `jj git push` is attempted, which is too late
   (push-app already succeeded in our case).

   **Policy (decided):** error loudly, with the exact
   `jj bookmark track <b> --remote=<r> -R <repo>` remediation
   command. No self-heal — keeps the fix explicit and visible.

   **Scope (every command that creates or mutates repo state):**
   - `vc-x1 init`: already correct (Step 10 calls `jj bookmark track`).
     Add the check as a post-condition sanity assertion anyway.
   - `vc-x1 clone`: does `git clone` then `jj git init --colocate`.
     Whether that combination auto-establishes tracking in jj's
     workspace store is unclear; probably needs an explicit
     `jj bookmark track` for each cloned bookmark after the init.
     Verify and fix.
   - `vc-x1 test-fixture`: Step 7's `jj git push --bookmark main`
     establishes tracking as a side effect of the first push. Works
     correctly — confirmed via `jj bookmark list --tracked` on a
     fresh fixture. No change needed; the post-condition sanity
     check would naturally cover it.
   - `vc-x1 sync` preflight: detect + error.
   - `vc-x1 push` preflight: detect + error (before any mutation).
   - `vc-x1 finalize`: detect + error before the squash, so a
     failed push doesn't leave a half-finalized state.

   Shared helper: `common::verify_tracking(repo, bookmark, remote)
   -> Result<(), Err>` or similar. Probably use
   `jj bookmark list --tracked -T <template>` under the hood
   rather than parsing human-readable output.
 - Allow `vc-x1 push` to work on code or bot repo together or independantly and
   specifically we should be able to "squash" in th code repo just as we do in
   the bot repo.
 - Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
 - Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
 - Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
 - Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
 - Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
 - Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
 - Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
 - Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
 - Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]
- push polish: --dry-run, --step, non-tty detection, gitignore warning (0.37.0-5) [48]
- push docs + workflow migration — CLAUDE.md rewrite + README section (0.37.0) [48]
- First-dogfood polish for push: editor template, gitignore-fatal, sync --check, log prefix, quieter subprocess (0.37.1) [53]

# References

[4]: /notes/chores-01.md#refactor-and-add-desc-subcommand
[5]: /notes/chores-01.md#claude-repo-issue-070-dev0-through-dev2
[27]: /notes/chores-02.md#0260--shell-completion-via-clap_complete-env

content.text:
All green. Summary of the 0.37.2 commit:

- `src/main.rs` — two new helpers (`track_probe`, `track_probe_one`) + entry/exit calls around the dispatch match. Uses `jj bookmark list --tracked main -R <repo>` and checks for `@origin:` line. Graceful degradation: `no-jj` when repo isn't there, `err(<reason>)` on subprocess failure. Skipped under `is_detached_exec`.
- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — `0.37.1` → `0.37.2`.
- `notes/chores-05.md` — new subsection with rationale, output shape, and a ripout checklist (three touch points, ~80 lines to revert).
- `notes/todo.md` — Done entry for 0.37.2 with `[55]` ref.

Smoke test in the app repo showed `tracked/tracked` both sides, as expected.

Work complete. Please review. On approval I'll prep the commit title and body for a second approval before pushing.

content.text:
You're right, this is ugly. Two things happened:

1. **The "Invalid tool parameters" you saw** — my first `Bash` tool call had a bogus `command_description` argument and was rejected. I retried; the second call went through. That part wasn't the problem.

2. **The real problem: stale push state.** Look at the output of the successful call — it ran *only* `finalize-claude`, then said "completed all stages (state cleared)":

   ```
   push finalize-claude: vc-x1 finalize --repo ...
   push: completed all stages (state cleared)
   ```

   The state file from the **previous push** (0.37.1's, which errored at finalize-claude before we recovered manually) was still sitting there saying "resume at finalize-claude". So this run resumed there, called finalize (no-op since we'd already finalized manually), and declared itself complete. **It never ran preflight / review / message / commit-app / commit-claude / bookmark-both / push-app.** Your 0.37.2 working copy changes are still uncommitted.

`jj st` confirms — all the edits still dirty:

```
M Cargo.lock
M Cargo.toml
M notes/chores-05.md
M notes/todo.md
M src/main.rs
```

**Root cause:** push's state file isn't cleared when the user recovers out-of-band (manual `vc-x1 finalize`). Push assumes the previous run's halt point is still its halt point, and resumes blindly.

**Fix for this session:** `--restart` to clear the stale state and run from scratch:

```bash
vc-x1 push main --restart --yes --title "$TITLE" --body "$BODY"
```

(I still have `$TITLE` and `$BODY` staged conceptually; would re-assemble the same strings.)

**Systemic followup (new todo):** push should detect stale state on resume. If the saved state's `claude_chid` / `app_chid` no longer match current `@-` or the bookmarks are already past those commits, warn or auto-clear and restart. Worth adding to `notes/todo.md`.

Want me to:
1. Add the new todo entry, then
2. Run the `--restart` push to actually commit 0.37.2?

message.content: Recovery is easy, but it seems to me our preflight is brittle and maybe error handling in general?

content.text:
Agreed, and the failures in this session trace back to one design gap. Concrete observations from what we've hit:

**Symptom 1 (0.37.1 push):** failed at `finalize-claude` with a real error (non-tracking bookmark). Push exited; state file parked at `finalize-claude`. But the outside world kept moving — we ran `vc-x1 finalize` manually, then `jj squash --ignore-immutable` + force-push to remove tf-1. Push knew nothing about any of that.

**Symptom 2 (0.37.2 push):** push resumed from the parked state, ran `finalize-claude` (which was a no-op because the world was already finalized), then cheerfully declared "completed all stages (state cleared)" — a lie in spirit. Every earlier stage (preflight, review, commit-app, …) was skipped despite a working copy full of pending changes that needed committing.

**Root cause:** push's state file is assumed authoritative. The state says "resume at X" and push does, without asking whether the repo actually *looks like* it's at stage X. State and reality can diverge through:
- Out-of-band recovery (manual `vc-x1 finalize` / `jj squash`)
- Force-push / history rewrite between runs
- Time passing + the user starting new work
- A previous run's "successful" finish that the state file missed

**Design direction for the systemic fix:**

1. **State-sanity preflight.** On resume, before any stage runs, verify the saved state's expected fingerprint matches reality: does `state.app_chid` still exist? Is `main` at `state.app_chid`'s commit? Are `state.claude_chid` and `state.bookmark` consistent with the repo's current state? If not, refuse with a loud "state is stale — run `vc-x1 push --restart`" error.

2. **Stage prerequisites.** Each stage declares what it expects (working-copy dirty for commit-app? bookmark at specific commit for bookmark-both? etc.). The dispatcher checks before running. Makes "this stage is actually a no-op because the work is done" detectable, not silently skipped.

3. **Honesty in completion.** "Completed all stages" should only print when stages genuinely ran or were verified-already-done, not when they were skipped without verification.

4. **Document the out-of-band recovery contract.** CLAUDE.md's "Manual finalize fallback" tells you how to run finalize by hand but doesn't say "and clear push's state after". That's a documentation gap too.

**Immediate practical path** (unchanged from my last message):
1. Add two new todos: (a) state-sanity preflight on resume, (b) stage-prereq verification + honest completion.
2. `vc-x1 push main --restart` to actually ship 0.37.2.

Green light for both?

message.content: y

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress


## Todo

A markdown list of task to do in the near feature

 - sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary (per-repo
   pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only fix shipped in
   0.37.1; this is the design+impl of the actual signal. [54]
 - Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both repos in
   one shot. Natural home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
 - Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards through to
   the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation). 0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`;
   `--no-check` would be the user-opt-in to "auto-rebase under the gates,
   I trust the sync state". Default stays `--check`.
 - `vc-x1 test-fixture` should refuse `--path` values that resolve inside
   the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood surfaced this:
   `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj snapshot the fixture's bare-git
   remotes into the commit — a 56-file noise blob that got force-push'd
   off the remote later. Tool-level prevention, not `.gitignore`.
 - Tighten CLAUDE.md "Late changes after push" recipe: drop the
   `jj bookmark set <bookmark> -r @- -R .` line when the squash target
   is `@-` (bookmark follows naturally). Keep it only for the cases
   where squash lands somewhere the bookmark isn't already.
 - Non-tracking-remote bookmark detection across every repo-modifying
   command. Diagnosed 2026-04-22 dogfood: jj's tracking state is
   **per-workspace** (local `.jj` store), not shared via git refs.
   Sync across machines transfers refs but not the tracking flag —
   so a fresh workspace fetched-into never auto-tracks. The failure
   surfaces only when `jj git push` is attempted, which is too late
   (push-app already succeeded in our case).

   **Policy (decided):** error loudly, with the exact
   `jj bookmark track <b> --remote=<r> -R <repo>` remediation
   command. No self-heal — keeps the fix explicit and visible.

   **Scope (every command that creates or mutates repo state):**
   - `vc-x1 init`: already correct (Step 10 calls `jj bookmark track`).
     Add the check as a post-condition sanity assertion anyway.
   - `vc-x1 clone`: does `git clone` then `jj git init --colocate`.
     Whether that combination auto-establishes tracking in jj's
     workspace store is unclear; probably needs an explicit
     `jj bookmark track` for each cloned bookmark after the init.
     Verify and fix.
   - `vc-x1 test-fixture`: Step 7's `jj git push --bookmark main`
     establishes tracking as a side effect of the first push. Works
     correctly — confirmed via `jj bookmark list --tracked` on a
     fresh fixture. No change needed; the post-condition sanity
     check would naturally cover it.
   - `vc-x1 sync` preflight: detect + error.
   - `vc-x1 push` preflight: detect + error (before any mutation).
   - `vc-x1 finalize`: detect + error before the squash, so a
     failed push doesn't leave a half-finalized state.

   Shared helper: `common::verify_tracking(repo, bookmark, remote)
   -> Result<(), Err>` or similar. Probably use
   `jj bookmark list --tracked -T <template>` under the hood
   rather than parsing human-readable output.
 - Allow `vc-x1 push` to work on code or bot repo together or independantly and
   specifically we should be able to "squash" in th code repo just as we do in
   the bot repo.
 - Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
 - Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
 - Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
 - Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
 - Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
 - Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
 - Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
 - Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
 - Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]
- push polish: --dry-run, --step, non-tty detection, gitignore warning (0.37.0-5) [48]
- push docs + workflow migration — CLAUDE.md rewrite + README section (0.37.0) [48]
- First-dogfood polish for push: editor template, gitignore-fatal, sync --check, log prefix, quieter subprocess (0.37.1) [53]
- Temporary bookmark-tracking diagnostic probe on command entry/exit (0.37.2) [55]

# References

[4]: /notes/chores-01.md#refactor-and-add-desc-subcommand
[5]: /notes/chores-01.md#claude-repo-issue-070-dev0-through-dev2
[27]: /notes/chores-02.md#0260--shell-completion-via-clap_complete-env
[28]: /notes/chores-02.md#testing-results
[29]: /notes/chores-02.md#shell-completion-discovery

[50]: /notes/chores-05.md#sync-improvements--single-repo-support--quieter-dry-run-0363
[51]: /notes/chores-05.md#test-harness-refactor-0362
[52]: /notes/chores-05.md#open-questions--tbd
[53]: /notes/chores-05.md#first-dogfood-polish-for-push-0371
[55]: /notes/chores-05.md#temporary-bookmark-tracking-diagnostic-probe-0372
[54]: /notes/chores-05.md#open--sync-up-to-date-should-mention-working-copy-state

content.text:
**The probe worked — caught the culprit in one run.** Look at the delta:

```
track-probe enter vc-x1 push: app(main)=tracked, .claude(main)=tracked
...
track-probe exit  vc-x1 push: app(main)=tracked, .claude(main)=NOT_TRACKED
```

`.claude(main)` went `tracked` → `NOT_TRACKED` **inside a single `vc-x1 push` invocation**. So the culprit is one of push's own stages, not an external event.

**Narrowing further** — push's parent process ran these stages against `.claude` before exiting:

1. `commit-claude`: `jj commit -R .claude`
2. `bookmark-both`: `jj bookmark set main -r @- -R .claude`
3. `finalize-claude`: spawned `vc-x1 finalize … --detach` (detached — parent doesn't wait; child has 10s delay before squash+push)

The 10s finalize delay means the detached child's squash+push hadn't started yet when the exit probe fired. So the tracking loss happened in stage 1 or 2 — almost certainly `jj commit` or `jj bookmark set`.

My bet: **`jj bookmark set main -r @- -R .claude`**. Moving a tracking bookmark to a commit that isn't a descendant of the tracked remote's tip can flag the bookmark as "moved away from its tracked remote" in jj, which some versions surface as dropping from the `--tracked` list even though the relationship still exists. Alternatively it could be `jj commit` if the working-copy's auto-advance interacts with the bookmark.

**Immediate data to confirm:**

```bash
jj op log -R /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/.claude --limit 10
jj bookmark list --all-remotes -R /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/.claude
```

The op log will show the exact op that changed bookmark state; the bookmark listing will show current relationship (is `main@origin` still present? is local `main` ahead?).

Want me to run those probes? Or will you?

One side note on the push itself: the commit-and-push parent operations all ran, so this run **did** land the 0.37.2 commit on the app remote. `.claude`'s detached finalize will have pushed (or errored) by now. The main visible failure is the tracking-probe-captured regression, not the push itself.

content.text:
Important plot twist — **I may have bugged the probe**, not caught a real regression. Look at the current state:

```
main: vqmlwnsr fa6e351e  ← local
  @git: vqmlwnsr fa6e351e
  @origin: vqmlwnsr fa6e351e  ← remote matches
```

Fully tracked, local and `@origin` at the same commit. That's clean state.

**But at the moment the exit probe fired**, the sequence was:
1. commit-claude made new commit `8fd3a69d…` / `07e3963d…` (id changed between ops, see op log)
2. bookmark-both moved local `main` to that new commit
3. **Probe fired here.** Local was at the new commit; `@origin` was still at the old (0.37.1) commit — local **ahead by one**.
4. Detached finalize's push caught `@origin` up; they rejoined.

My probe parses the output of `jj bookmark list --tracked main -R <repo>` and checks for a line starting with `@origin:`. But jj decorates the `@origin` line with sync state when local is ahead — something like:

```
  @origin (ahead by 1 commits): <old_commit>
```

That doesn't start with `@origin:` — it starts with `@origin (ahead…`. So my `starts_with("@origin:")` missed it. **False positive: probe reported NOT_TRACKED when tracking was actually fine, just ahead.**

This matches the op log: the local `jj bookmark set` + (later) `jj git push` sequence is exactly what creates the briefly-ahead-then-rejoined pattern. Tracking never actually dropped.

**Fix for the probe:** loosen the marker to `@origin` (no colon), or better yet query tracking status via jj's template language directly — something like `jj bookmark list --tracked main -T 'if(self.remote(), "tracked", "not-tracked")'` — instead of parsing human-readable output.

Small follow-up edit. Want me to fix the probe and re-test, so we can tell whether there's a real tracking-loss bug hiding anywhere, or if every "NOT_TRACKED" we've seen from the probe was just my parsing glitch?

content.text:
Second probe bug caught by the same probe — and it's mine, not jj's.

You ran from inside `.claude`. My probe uses hardcoded relative paths `.` and `.claude`:

- `.` from inside `.claude` → `/…/vc-x1/.claude` (HAS `.jj`, so labeled "app(main)=tracked" — but that's actually `.claude`'s tracking being reported as "app")
- `.claude` from inside `.claude` → `/…/vc-x1/.claude/.claude` (doesn't exist → `no-jj`)

So both labels are lying depending on cwd. Probe needs to find the **workspace root** (walking up looking for `.vc-config.toml` or similar) before probing, not blindly use cwd-relative paths.

So the probe has **two bugs** to fix:

1. Strict `@origin:` marker parse misses the "local-ahead" decorated form.
2. cwd-relative paths mislabel and miss when cwd isn't the workspace root.

Let me find the workspace-root helper and fix both.

47	/// because its own `about` already carries the name+version+tagline.
48	/// Walks via `mut_subcommand` so individual subcommand
49	/// `#[command(long_about = ...)]` blocks don't have to repeat the
50	/// banner text.
51	fn cli_with_banner() -> clap::Command {
52	    fn add_to_subs(mut cmd: clap::Command) -> clap::Command {
53	        let names: Vec<String> = cmd
54	            .get_subcommands()
55	            .map(|c| c.get_name().to_string())
56	            .collect();
57	        for name in names {
58	            cmd = cmd.mut_subcommand(name, add_with_banner);
59	        }
60	        cmd
61	    }
62	    fn add_with_banner(mut cmd: clap::Command) -> clap::Command {
63	        cmd = cmd.before_help(BANNER);
64	        let names: Vec<String> = cmd
65	            .get_subcommands()
66	            .map(|c| c.get_name().to_string())
67	            .collect();
68	        for name in names {
69	            cmd = cmd.mut_subcommand(name, add_with_banner);
70	        }
71	        cmd
72	    }
73	    add_to_subs(Cli::command())
74	}

Net removal: ~80 lines.

## Fix two probe bugs (0.37.3)

First use of the 0.37.2 probe caught two bugs — in the probe itself,
not in vc-x1's push flow.

**Bug A — cwd-relative paths mislabel and miss.** The probe hardcoded
`Path::new(".")` and `Path::new(".claude")` as the repo paths. Run
from inside `.claude`, those resolve to `.claude` and `.claude/.claude`
respectively — probe reported `app(main)=tracked, .claude(main)=no-jj`,
which is doubly wrong: the "app" column is actually showing `.claude`'s
status, and the ".claude" column is showing a nonexistent path. Fix:
walk up from cwd looking for `.vc-config.toml` with `path = "/"`.
That file marks the app-repo side of the dual-repo pair, so its
containing dir is the workspace root. Probe `<root>` and
`<root>/.claude` unconditionally — gives consistent labeling from
any cwd inside the workspace. Outside any workspace, probe prints
`no-workspace` and skips.

**Bug B — `@origin:` parse misses the divergent-decorated form.**
Probe checked `line.trim_start().starts_with("@origin:")`. But when
local `main` is ahead of `@origin`, jj decorates the line as
`@origin (ahead by 1 commits): <old_commit>` — same tracking
relationship, different prefix, my check fails. **This is what
produced the scary-looking `NOT_TRACKED` at 0.37.2's push-exit
probe** — the bookmark WAS tracking, it was just briefly
local-ahead between `bookmark-both` and the detached finalize's
push. False positive. Fix: accept both `@origin:` (synced) and
`@origin ` (divergent-decorated, note trailing space) as tracking
markers. The tracking relationship is what matters; sync-state is
orthogonal.

- `src/main.rs` — `track_probe` rewritten to call
  `find_workspace_root()` first, then probe `<root>` + `<root>/.claude`.
  Graceful fallback to `no-workspace` when cwd is outside any vc-x1
  workspace.
- `src/main.rs` — new `find_workspace_root()` helper. Walks up from
  cwd reading each `.vc-config.toml` it finds; matches if the file
  has `path = "/"` (with or without surrounding whitespace). Returns
  `None` at filesystem root if no match found.
- `src/main.rs` — `track_probe_one` tracking detection loosened.
  Now accepts either `@{remote}:` or `@{remote} ` (trailing space
  for the decorated form). Doc comment expanded to call out both
  cases.
- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — `0.37.2` → `0.37.3`.

Implication for the chain of diagnoses: the `NOT_TRACKED` seen at
0.37.2's push-exit probe was a false positive. There is no evidence
yet of an actual tracking-loss bug inside vc-x1's push flow. The
*original* tracking loss that triggered the 0.37.1 finalize-claude
failure was real (`jj git push` genuinely refused), but that loss
happened before any probe existed — origin lies somewhere in
`.claude`'s historical bootstrap, not in the recent commit+push
sequence. The probe continues to provide value going forward: it
would catch any new tracking-loss the moment it happens.

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress


## Todo

A markdown list of task to do in the near feature

 - sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary (per-repo
   pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only fix shipped in
   0.37.1; this is the design+impl of the actual signal. [54]
 - Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both repos in
   one shot. Natural home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
 - Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards through to
   the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation). 0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`;
   `--no-check` would be the user-opt-in to "auto-rebase under the gates,
   I trust the sync state". Default stays `--check`.
 - `vc-x1 push`: state-sanity preflight on resume. Before any stage runs,
   verify saved state matches reality: `state.app_chid` still exists
   (not abandoned/rewritten)?  `main` bookmark at `state.app_chid`'s
   commit? `state.claude_chid` consistent with `.claude` working copy?
   On mismatch, refuse with a loud "state is stale — run `vc-x1 push
   --restart`" error. Surfaced 2026-04-22: 0.37.1 push errored at
   finalize-claude; out-of-band recovery (manual finalize + force-push
   of a squash) moved the world forward; 0.37.2 push then resumed at
   the parked finalize-claude, no-op'd, and falsely declared "completed
   all stages" while working copies still held uncommitted changes.
 - `vc-x1 push`: stage-prereq verification + honest completion. Each
   stage declares what it expects (working-copy dirty for commit-app;
   bookmark at specific commit for bookmark-both; etc.); dispatcher
   checks before running. "Completed all stages" should only print
   when stages genuinely ran or were verified-already-done, not when
   they were skipped without verification. Same dogfood surfaced this.
 - CLAUDE.md "Manual finalize fallback": document that push's state
   file must be cleared (`vc-x1 push --restart` or `rm`) after any
   out-of-band recovery (manual `vc-x1 finalize`, `jj squash
   --ignore-immutable` + force-push, etc.) so a later `vc-x1 push`
   doesn't resume from a now-stale halt point.
 - `vc-x1 test-fixture` should refuse `--path` values that resolve inside
   the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood surfaced this:
   `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj snapshot the fixture's bare-git
   remotes into the commit — a 56-file noise blob that got force-push'd
   off the remote later. Tool-level prevention, not `.gitignore`.
 - Tighten CLAUDE.md "Late changes after push" recipe: drop the
   `jj bookmark set <bookmark> -r @- -R .` line when the squash target
   is `@-` (bookmark follows naturally). Keep it only for the cases
   where squash lands somewhere the bookmark isn't already.
 - Non-tracking-remote bookmark detection across every repo-modifying
   command. Diagnosed 2026-04-22 dogfood: jj's tracking state is
   **per-workspace** (local `.jj` store), not shared via git refs.
   Sync across machines transfers refs but not the tracking flag —
   so a fresh workspace fetched-into never auto-tracks. The failure
   surfaces only when `jj git push` is attempted, which is too late
   (push-app already succeeded in our case).

   **Policy (decided):** error loudly, with the exact
   `jj bookmark track <b> --remote=<r> -R <repo>` remediation
   command. No self-heal — keeps the fix explicit and visible.

   **Scope (every command that creates or mutates repo state):**
   - `vc-x1 init`: already correct (Step 10 calls `jj bookmark track`).
     Add the check as a post-condition sanity assertion anyway.
   - `vc-x1 clone`: does `git clone` then `jj git init --colocate`.
     Whether that combination auto-establishes tracking in jj's
     workspace store is unclear; probably needs an explicit
     `jj bookmark track` for each cloned bookmark after the init.
     Verify and fix.
   - `vc-x1 test-fixture`: Step 7's `jj git push --bookmark main`
     establishes tracking as a side effect of the first push. Works
     correctly — confirmed via `jj bookmark list --tracked` on a
     fresh fixture. No change needed; the post-condition sanity
     check would naturally cover it.
   - `vc-x1 sync` preflight: detect + error.
   - `vc-x1 push` preflight: detect + error (before any mutation).
   - `vc-x1 finalize`: detect + error before the squash, so a
     failed push doesn't leave a half-finalized state.

   Shared helper: `common::verify_tracking(repo, bookmark, remote)
   -> Result<(), Err>` or similar. Probably use
   `jj bookmark list --tracked -T <template>` under the hood
   rather than parsing human-readable output.
 - Allow `vc-x1 push` to work on code or bot repo together or independantly and
   specifically we should be able to "squash" in th code repo just as we do in
   the bot repo.
 - Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
 - Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
 - Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
 - Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
 - Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
 - Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
 - Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
 - Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
 - Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]
