toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress


## Todo

A markdown list of task to do in the near feature

 - sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary (per-repo
   pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only fix shipped in
   0.37.1; this is the design+impl of the actual signal. [54]
 - Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both repos in
   one shot. Natural home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
 - Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards through to
   the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation). 0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`;
   `--no-check` would be the user-opt-in to "auto-rebase under the gates,
   I trust the sync state". Default stays `--check`.
 - `vc-x1 push`: state-sanity preflight on resume. Before any stage runs,
   verify saved state matches reality: `state.app_chid` still exists
   (not abandoned/rewritten)?  `main` bookmark at `state.app_chid`'s
   commit? `state.claude_chid` consistent with `.claude` working copy?
   On mismatch, refuse with a loud "state is stale — run `vc-x1 push
   --restart`" error. Surfaced 2026-04-22: 0.37.1 push errored at
   finalize-claude; out-of-band recovery (manual finalize + force-push
   of a squash) moved the world forward; 0.37.2 push then resumed at
   the parked finalize-claude, no-op'd, and falsely declared "completed
   all stages" while working copies still held uncommitted changes.
 - `vc-x1 push`: stage-prereq verification + honest completion. Each
   stage declares what it expects (working-copy dirty for commit-app;
   bookmark at specific commit for bookmark-both; etc.); dispatcher
   checks before running. "Completed all stages" should only print
   when stages genuinely ran or were verified-already-done, not when
   they were skipped without verification. Same dogfood surfaced this.
 - CLAUDE.md "Manual finalize fallback": document that push's state
   file must be cleared (`vc-x1 push --restart` or `rm`) after any
   out-of-band recovery (manual `vc-x1 finalize`, `jj squash
   --ignore-immutable` + force-push, etc.) so a later `vc-x1 push`
   doesn't resume from a now-stale halt point.
 - `vc-x1 test-fixture` should refuse `--path` values that resolve inside
   the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood surfaced this:
   `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj snapshot the fixture's bare-git
   remotes into the commit — a 56-file noise blob that got force-push'd
   off the remote later. Tool-level prevention, not `.gitignore`.
 - Tighten CLAUDE.md "Late changes after push" recipe: drop the
   `jj bookmark set <bookmark> -r @- -R .` line when the squash target
   is `@-` (bookmark follows naturally). Keep it only for the cases
   where squash lands somewhere the bookmark isn't already.
 - Non-tracking-remote bookmark detection across every repo-modifying
   command. Diagnosed 2026-04-22 dogfood: jj's tracking state is
   **per-workspace** (local `.jj` store), not shared via git refs.
   Sync across machines transfers refs but not the tracking flag —
   so a fresh workspace fetched-into never auto-tracks. The failure
   surfaces only when `jj git push` is attempted, which is too late
   (push-app already succeeded in our case).

   **Policy (decided):** error loudly, with the exact
   `jj bookmark track <b> --remote=<r> -R <repo>` remediation
   command. No self-heal — keeps the fix explicit and visible.

   **Scope (every command that creates or mutates repo state):**
   - `vc-x1 init`: already correct (Step 10 calls `jj bookmark track`).
     Add the check as a post-condition sanity assertion anyway.
   - `vc-x1 clone`: does `git clone` then `jj git init --colocate`.
     Whether that combination auto-establishes tracking in jj's
     workspace store is unclear; probably needs an explicit
     `jj bookmark track` for each cloned bookmark after the init.
     Verify and fix.
   - `vc-x1 test-fixture`: Step 7's `jj git push --bookmark main`
     establishes tracking as a side effect of the first push. Works
     correctly — confirmed via `jj bookmark list --tracked` on a
     fresh fixture. No change needed; the post-condition sanity
     check would naturally cover it.
   - `vc-x1 sync` preflight: detect + error.
   - `vc-x1 push` preflight: detect + error (before any mutation).
   - `vc-x1 finalize`: detect + error before the squash, so a
     failed push doesn't leave a half-finalized state.

   Shared helper: `common::verify_tracking(repo, bookmark, remote)
   -> Result<(), Err>` or similar. Probably use
   `jj bookmark list --tracked -T <template>` under the hood
   rather than parsing human-readable output.
 - Allow `vc-x1 push` to work on code or bot repo together or independantly and
   specifically we should be able to "squash" in th code repo just as we do in
   the bot repo.
 - Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
 - Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
 - Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
 - Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
 - Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
 - Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
 - Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
 - Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
 - Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

content.text:
All green. Proposed commit:

**Title** (35 chars):

```
fix: tracking-probe bugs (0.37.3)
```

**Body**:

```
First use of 0.37.2's tracking probe caught two bugs — in the
probe itself, not in vc-x1's push flow. Notable corollary: the
scary `NOT_TRACKED` at 0.37.2 push exit was a false positive
(Bug B below), not a real tracking-loss regression.

Bug A — cwd-relative paths mislabel and miss. Hardcoded `.` and
`.claude` as repo paths; run from inside `.claude` they
resolved wrong. Fix: walk up from cwd for `.vc-config.toml`
with `path = "/"` and probe <root> + <root>/.claude.

Bug B — `@origin:` parse misses divergent-decorated form. When
local is ahead, jj writes `@origin (ahead by N commits):`; my
starts_with("@origin:") missed it. Fix: accept both the synced
form and the decorated form as tracking markers.

- src/main.rs: track_probe rewritten to use find_workspace_root();
  new find_workspace_root helper (walks up, reads .vc-config.toml,
  matches `path = "/"`); track_probe_one tracking check loosened
  to accept `@origin:` and `@origin ` prefixes
- Cargo.toml / Cargo.lock: 0.37.2 → 0.37.3
- notes/chores-05.md: new 0.37.3 subsection
- notes/todo.md: Done line + ref [56]
```

OK to run `vc-x1 push main --yes ...` with this title/body?

message.content: So both bug A and B were operator errors in some sense and we haven't really found "the" problem?

content.text:
You're exactly right, and it's worth saying out loud: **we still haven't identified what broke `.claude`'s tracking in the first place.**

What we actually have:

- **Real recovery.** The 0.37.1 push failure was genuine — `jj git push` refused because tracking was missing on this workspace. We fixed it with `jj bookmark track`.
- **Real diagnosis of the structural truth.** Tracking is per-workspace, not shared via git refs. This is useful system knowledge but it's not *why* tracking was missing on this particular workspace.
- **Two probe bugs fixed** — both mine. Zero evidence of vc-x1 itself breaking tracking captured by the (now-correct) probe.

What we **don't** have:

- A reproducible trigger.
- A confirmed commit/operation where tracking was dropped.
- Any idea whether the bug is historical (one-time setup mishap that's long since healed) or latent (waiting for specific conditions).

Two likely profiles for the original loss, in descending likelihood:

1. **Historical artifact.** `.claude` on 3900x was bootstrapped at some past point — possibly before `init.rs:482-485` ever added the `jj bookmark track` call, possibly by an ad-hoc manual `jj git init --colocate` + `jj git fetch`, possibly by an old `vc-x1 init`. That one-time miss persisted until we pushed from this machine for the first time. Op log's oldest entry (23 hours ago) doesn't go back far enough to confirm.

2. **Latent bug we haven't triggered.** Some specific vc-x1 operation (or a specific state under which it runs) drops tracking, and we just haven't repeated the conditions. The now-correct probe would catch this, but nothing we've done since has caught it.

Options from here:

- **Leave the probe in through more dogfood**, hope it catches a repeat. Low cost; requires patience and a repro.
- **Rip the probe now** (we haven't caught anything in several clean runs), accept the original loss as historical, and rely on the "detect-and-error in sync/push preflight" todo as the real protection against any future recurrence.
- **Actively try to reproduce**: bootstrap a fresh `.claude` via various setup paths (historical init, `jj git init --colocate` + fetch, etc.) and see which produces a non-tracking bookmark by default. If one does, that's probably what happened here.

My lean: option 3 as a small follow-up (hour or two of systematic testing), then either the probe catches something or we retire it with a clear "historical — can't reproduce" note and move on. No point keeping a diagnostic in place indefinitely when we've had multiple clean runs.

Your call — happy to leave it, rip it, or try to reproduce. What's the cadence you want?

message.content: I've had it happen more than once I suggest we keep the entry/exit checks in, I don't think they can hurt us, IMO

content.text:
Agree it's worth keeping — "happens more than once" is the key data point I didn't have. The probe's cheap and the signal is valuable. Two refinements to consider though:

**1. Promote from "temporary" to "permanent" in docs.** The 0.37.2 chores entry frames it as temporary with a ripout checklist. If we're keeping it, that framing is misleading for future readers. Small doc edit: reframe as a permanent sanity check, drop the ripout section.

**2. Quieter when clean?** Right now the probe outputs *two* lines per command regardless of state:

```
track-probe enter vc-x1 chid: app(main)=tracked, .claude(main)=tracked
track-probe exit  vc-x1 chid: app(main)=tracked, .claude(main)=tracked
```

Every command, every time. After what we did in 0.37.1 to quiet subprocess output, this stands out. Alternative behavior:

- **Silent when clean** — always probe both entry and exit, but only *print* when:
  (a) entry reports anything other than "all tracked", OR
  (b) exit differs from entry (the probe catches a change)

  Net effect: clean runs stay silent (zero lines), problem runs are loud (loud error-level line naming the command that caused the change).

- Keep always-on output (your current suggestion) — full transparency, two lines/command forever.

My lean: **silent-when-clean preserves the full detection value and removes the steady noise**. The probe fires on every command; the output only appears when there's something to say. That's the pattern most UNIX-y tools converge on ("diff of nothing is nothing").

But this is your system and your signal preference. Happy either way. Want:

- (a) Keep both bugs fixed as-is from 0.37.3 (verbose every command) + tiny doc reframing in a 0.37.4, or
- (b) Refine to silent-when-clean + doc reframing together in 0.37.4?

message.content: I vote keeping it as is for now and gain confidence that the "signal" is solid.

content.text:
Good discipline — don't touch the signal while you're still calibrating trust in it.

So 0.37.4 is a tiny doc-only reframing:

- `src/main.rs` — `track_probe` doc comment: drop "TEMPORARY" and "Rip out once the culprit is identified". Replace with a neutral "permanent sanity check; stays verbose until signal confidence is established, then consider quieter-when-clean refinement" note.
- `notes/chores-05.md` — new 0.37.4 subsection that amends 0.37.2's "temporary diagnostic" framing to "permanent (pending quiet-mode refinement)". Note the "happened more than once" data point as the reason to keep it.
- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — `0.37.3` → `0.37.4`.
- `notes/todo.md` — Done line + new Todo entry for the silent-when-clean refinement (captured but deferred).

No code behavior change. Pre-commit checklist should be trivial. Green light?

lines: -/// TEMPORARY — diagnostic probe for the non-tracking-bookmark issue
-/// surfaced during the 0.37.1 dogfood. Prints `main` bookmark tracking
-/// status in both repos of the dual-repo workspace on entry and exit
-/// of every command. If entry and exit differ, the executing command
-/// is the culprit; if entry is `NOT_TRACKED` but a previous command's

+/// Permanent sanity check for the `main`-bookmark tracking state
+/// in both repos of the dual-repo workspace. Prints one line on

Implication for the chain of diagnoses: the `NOT_TRACKED` seen at
0.37.2's push-exit probe was a false positive. There is no evidence
yet of an actual tracking-loss bug inside vc-x1's push flow. The
*original* tracking loss that triggered the 0.37.1 finalize-claude
failure was real (`jj git push` genuinely refused), but that loss
happened before any probe existed — origin lies somewhere in
`.claude`'s historical bootstrap, not in the recent commit+push
sequence. The probe continues to provide value going forward: it
would catch any new tracking-loss the moment it happens.

## Promote track-probe from temporary to permanent (0.37.4)

User data point after the 0.37.3 bug fixes: "I've had it happen
more than once." That reframes the probe from "one-shot
investigation" (add, catch, rip out) to "permanent sanity check"
(always on, signals a real-but-rare class of failures).

Amendment to the 0.37.2 + 0.37.3 framing, not code behavior —
the probe itself is unchanged, only its docs and status.

- `src/main.rs` — `track_probe` doc comment rewritten. Drops
  "TEMPORARY" and the "Rip out once the culprit is identified"
  line. Adds context on why it stayed in place (multiple past
  occurrences reported) and notes the deferred "silent when
  clean" refinement that would preserve detection value while
  removing steady-state noise.
- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — `0.37.3` → `0.37.4`.
- `notes/todo.md` — Done line + new Todo for the quieter-mode
  refinement (silent when clean, loud on mismatch).

The ripout checklist in 0.37.2's subsection and the references
to "temporary" in 0.37.3's subsection are preserved as historical
record rather than edited in place — they accurately describe
the authors' intent *at the time those versions shipped*, and
rewriting them would erase the story of how the decision
evolved. Future readers find the authoritative status here
(subsection 0.37.4) via the normal chronological read.

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress


## Todo

A markdown list of task to do in the near feature

 - sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary (per-repo
   pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only fix shipped in
   0.37.1; this is the design+impl of the actual signal. [54]
 - Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both repos in
   one shot. Natural home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
 - Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards through to
   the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation). 0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`;
   `--no-check` would be the user-opt-in to "auto-rebase under the gates,
   I trust the sync state". Default stays `--check`.
 - `vc-x1 push`: state-sanity preflight on resume. Before any stage runs,
   verify saved state matches reality: `state.app_chid` still exists
   (not abandoned/rewritten)?  `main` bookmark at `state.app_chid`'s
   commit? `state.claude_chid` consistent with `.claude` working copy?
   On mismatch, refuse with a loud "state is stale — run `vc-x1 push
   --restart`" error. Surfaced 2026-04-22: 0.37.1 push errored at
   finalize-claude; out-of-band recovery (manual finalize + force-push
   of a squash) moved the world forward; 0.37.2 push then resumed at
   the parked finalize-claude, no-op'd, and falsely declared "completed
   all stages" while working copies still held uncommitted changes.
 - `vc-x1 push`: stage-prereq verification + honest completion. Each
   stage declares what it expects (working-copy dirty for commit-app;
   bookmark at specific commit for bookmark-both; etc.); dispatcher
   checks before running. "Completed all stages" should only print
   when stages genuinely ran or were verified-already-done, not when
   they were skipped without verification. Same dogfood surfaced this.
 - CLAUDE.md "Manual finalize fallback": document that push's state
   file must be cleared (`vc-x1 push --restart` or `rm`) after any
   out-of-band recovery (manual `vc-x1 finalize`, `jj squash
   --ignore-immutable` + force-push, etc.) so a later `vc-x1 push`
   doesn't resume from a now-stale halt point.
 - `vc-x1 test-fixture` should refuse `--path` values that resolve inside
   the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood surfaced this:
   `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj snapshot the fixture's bare-git
   remotes into the commit — a 56-file noise blob that got force-push'd
   off the remote later. Tool-level prevention, not `.gitignore`.
 - Tighten CLAUDE.md "Late changes after push" recipe: drop the
   `jj bookmark set <bookmark> -r @- -R .` line when the squash target
   is `@-` (bookmark follows naturally). Keep it only for the cases
   where squash lands somewhere the bookmark isn't already.
 - Non-tracking-remote bookmark detection across every repo-modifying
   command. Diagnosed 2026-04-22 dogfood: jj's tracking state is
   **per-workspace** (local `.jj` store), not shared via git refs.
   Sync across machines transfers refs but not the tracking flag —
   so a fresh workspace fetched-into never auto-tracks. The failure
   surfaces only when `jj git push` is attempted, which is too late
   (push-app already succeeded in our case).

   **Policy (decided):** error loudly, with the exact
   `jj bookmark track <b> --remote=<r> -R <repo>` remediation
   command. No self-heal — keeps the fix explicit and visible.

   **Scope (every command that creates or mutates repo state):**
   - `vc-x1 init`: already correct (Step 10 calls `jj bookmark track`).
     Add the check as a post-condition sanity assertion anyway.
   - `vc-x1 clone`: does `git clone` then `jj git init --colocate`.
     Whether that combination auto-establishes tracking in jj's
     workspace store is unclear; probably needs an explicit
     `jj bookmark track` for each cloned bookmark after the init.
     Verify and fix.
   - `vc-x1 test-fixture`: Step 7's `jj git push --bookmark main`
     establishes tracking as a side effect of the first push. Works
     correctly — confirmed via `jj bookmark list --tracked` on a
     fresh fixture. No change needed; the post-condition sanity
     check would naturally cover it.
   - `vc-x1 sync` preflight: detect + error.
   - `vc-x1 push` preflight: detect + error (before any mutation).
   - `vc-x1 finalize`: detect + error before the squash, so a
     failed push doesn't leave a half-finalized state.

   Shared helper: `common::verify_tracking(repo, bookmark, remote)
   -> Result<(), Err>` or similar. Probably use
   `jj bookmark list --tracked -T <template>` under the hood
   rather than parsing human-readable output.
 - Allow `vc-x1 push` to work on code or bot repo together or independantly and
   specifically we should be able to "squash" in th code repo just as we do in
   the bot repo.
 - Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
 - Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
 - Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
 - Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
 - Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
 - Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
 - Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
 - Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
 - Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]
- push polish: --dry-run, --step, non-tty detection, gitignore warning (0.37.0-5) [48]
- push docs + workflow migration — CLAUDE.md rewrite + README section (0.37.0) [48]
- First-dogfood polish for push: editor template, gitignore-fatal, sync --check, log prefix, quieter subprocess (0.37.1) [53]
- Temporary bookmark-tracking diagnostic probe on command entry/exit (0.37.2) [55]
- Fix two probe bugs: cwd-relative paths, strict @origin: parse (0.37.3) [56]

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress


## Todo

A markdown list of task to do in the near feature

 - sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary (per-repo
   pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only fix shipped in
   0.37.1; this is the design+impl of the actual signal. [54]
 - Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both repos in
   one shot. Natural home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
 - Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards through to
   the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation). 0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`;
   `--no-check` would be the user-opt-in to "auto-rebase under the gates,
   I trust the sync state". Default stays `--check`.
 - track-probe "silent when clean" refinement. Always probe on entry
   and exit, but only *print* when the state isn't fully tracked or
   the exit state differs from entry. Preserves detection value,
   removes the steady-state two-lines-per-command noise. Deferred
   in 0.37.4 until signal-confidence is established through more
   varied dogfood — changing output behavior before then would
   muddy the "is the probe itself reliable?" question.
 - `vc-x1 push`: state-sanity preflight on resume. Before any stage runs,
   verify saved state matches reality: `state.app_chid` still exists
   (not abandoned/rewritten)?  `main` bookmark at `state.app_chid`'s
   commit? `state.claude_chid` consistent with `.claude` working copy?
   On mismatch, refuse with a loud "state is stale — run `vc-x1 push
   --restart`" error. Surfaced 2026-04-22: 0.37.1 push errored at
   finalize-claude; out-of-band recovery (manual finalize + force-push
   of a squash) moved the world forward; 0.37.2 push then resumed at
   the parked finalize-claude, no-op'd, and falsely declared "completed
   all stages" while working copies still held uncommitted changes.
 - `vc-x1 push`: stage-prereq verification + honest completion. Each
   stage declares what it expects (working-copy dirty for commit-app;
   bookmark at specific commit for bookmark-both; etc.); dispatcher
   checks before running. "Completed all stages" should only print
   when stages genuinely ran or were verified-already-done, not when
   they were skipped without verification. Same dogfood surfaced this.
 - CLAUDE.md "Manual finalize fallback": document that push's state
   file must be cleared (`vc-x1 push --restart` or `rm`) after any
   out-of-band recovery (manual `vc-x1 finalize`, `jj squash
   --ignore-immutable` + force-push, etc.) so a later `vc-x1 push`
   doesn't resume from a now-stale halt point.
 - `vc-x1 test-fixture` should refuse `--path` values that resolve inside
   the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood surfaced this:
   `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj snapshot the fixture's bare-git
   remotes into the commit — a 56-file noise blob that got force-push'd
   off the remote later. Tool-level prevention, not `.gitignore`.
 - Tighten CLAUDE.md "Late changes after push" recipe: drop the
   `jj bookmark set <bookmark> -r @- -R .` line when the squash target
   is `@-` (bookmark follows naturally). Keep it only for the cases
   where squash lands somewhere the bookmark isn't already.
 - Non-tracking-remote bookmark detection across every repo-modifying
   command. Diagnosed 2026-04-22 dogfood: jj's tracking state is
   **per-workspace** (local `.jj` store), not shared via git refs.
   Sync across machines transfers refs but not the tracking flag —
   so a fresh workspace fetched-into never auto-tracks. The failure
   surfaces only when `jj git push` is attempted, which is too late
   (push-app already succeeded in our case).

   **Policy (decided):** error loudly, with the exact
   `jj bookmark track <b> --remote=<r> -R <repo>` remediation
   command. No self-heal — keeps the fix explicit and visible.

   **Scope (every command that creates or mutates repo state):**
   - `vc-x1 init`: already correct (Step 10 calls `jj bookmark track`).
     Add the check as a post-condition sanity assertion anyway.
   - `vc-x1 clone`: does `git clone` then `jj git init --colocate`.
     Whether that combination auto-establishes tracking in jj's
     workspace store is unclear; probably needs an explicit
     `jj bookmark track` for each cloned bookmark after the init.
     Verify and fix.
   - `vc-x1 test-fixture`: Step 7's `jj git push --bookmark main`
     establishes tracking as a side effect of the first push. Works
     correctly — confirmed via `jj bookmark list --tracked` on a
     fresh fixture. No change needed; the post-condition sanity
     check would naturally cover it.
   - `vc-x1 sync` preflight: detect + error.
   - `vc-x1 push` preflight: detect + error (before any mutation).
   - `vc-x1 finalize`: detect + error before the squash, so a
     failed push doesn't leave a half-finalized state.

   Shared helper: `common::verify_tracking(repo, bookmark, remote)
   -> Result<(), Err>` or similar. Probably use
   `jj bookmark list --tracked -T <template>` under the hood
   rather than parsing human-readable output.
 - Allow `vc-x1 push` to work on code or bot repo together or independantly and
   specifically we should be able to "squash" in th code repo just as we do in
   the bot repo.
 - Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
 - Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
 - Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
 - Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
 - Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
 - Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
 - Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
 - Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
 - Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]
- push polish: --dry-run, --step, non-tty detection, gitignore warning (0.37.0-5) [48]
- push docs + workflow migration — CLAUDE.md rewrite + README section (0.37.0) [48]
- First-dogfood polish for push: editor template, gitignore-fatal, sync --check, log prefix, quieter subprocess (0.37.1) [53]
- Temporary bookmark-tracking diagnostic probe on command entry/exit (0.37.2) [55]
- Fix two probe bugs: cwd-relative paths, strict @origin: parse (0.37.3) [56]
- Promote track-probe from temporary to permanent (0.37.4) [57]

content.text:
All green. Proposed commit:

**Title** (42 chars):

```
docs: promote track-probe to permanent (0.37.4)
```

**Body**:

```
User data point after 0.37.3: "I've had it happen more than
once." That reframes the probe from one-shot investigation
to permanent sanity check. Docs only; no code behavior change.

- src/main.rs: track_probe doc comment drops "TEMPORARY" and
  "Rip out once the culprit is identified". Adds context on
  why it's permanent (multiple past occurrences) and notes
  the deferred "silent when clean" refinement.
- Cargo.toml / Cargo.lock: 0.37.3 → 0.37.4
- notes/chores-05.md: new 0.37.4 subsection amending the
  0.37.2 + 0.37.3 "temporary" framing. Historical subsections
  left intact as a record of how intent evolved.
- notes/todo.md: Done line + new Todo for the silent-when-clean
  refinement (deferred until signal-confidence is established)
```

OK to run `vc-x1 push main --yes ...`?

message.content: We'll be removing the always on entry/exit after we can confidence and it'll be failure only in the near future, or are you talking about something else?

content.text:
Same thing, yes — I called it "silent when clean" but that's identical to your "failure only". Behavior intent:

- **Now (0.37.4)**: always print entry + exit. No change from 0.37.3.
- **Later (some 0.37.N)**: always probe, but only print on `NOT_TRACKED` or `entry != exit`. Silent in the common clean case.

The todo entry captures that, and your earlier "keep as-is for now and gain confidence" is what gates the transition — we'll refine once you trust the signal.

So 0.37.4 is *just* the doc reframing (promote from temporary to permanent, drop the ripout-checklist language). No behavior change. Same proposed commit as above.

OK to push?

message.content: One thought, I think when we hit the entry=ok exit=failed we should "save" the entry state so we can display it on during exit just so we're 100% positive not 99% it'd be if we say it didn't fire so it was good incoming.

toolUseResult.newString:
- track-probe "silent when clean" refinement. Always probe on entry
   and exit, but only *print* when the state isn't fully tracked or
   the exit state differs from entry. **When we do print on exit,
   include the entry state in the output** so the transition is
   explicit rather than inferred — "entry was ok (not printed), exit
   failed" is inference; "entry=tracked, exit=NOT_TRACKED" in one
   printed line is evidence. Record entry state in a local variable
   at the enter call site and pass into the exit call. Output shape:
   `track-probe vc-x1 <cmd>: enter=<state> → exit=<state>` — one
   line only when there's something to say. Preserves detection
   value, removes the steady-state two-lines-per-command noise, and
   keeps provenance unambiguous. Deferred in 0.37.4 until
   signal-confidence is established through more varied dogfood —
   changing output behavior before then would muddy the "is the
   probe itself reliable?" question.

Let me update the todo entry to reflect this before we commit 0.37.4. The change is to the future-refinement spec, not to 0.37.4's behavior.

message.content: Sorry, why do you call it "track-probe"

content.text:
It's just the log-prefix string I picked when I sketched the probe earlier — "track" for bookmark tracking, "probe" as in diagnostic instrument. It's arbitrary naming. I chose it for:

- Short (one word-pair), visible at a glance in output
- Specific enough to grep for (`grep track-probe` or `grep -v track-probe`)
- Says "this is telling you about tracking"
- Says "this is an inserted observer, not part of the actual work"

Alternatives if you prefer:

- `tracking:` — simpler, but ambiguous with other "tracking" concepts
- `track-check:` — emphasizes verify-step rather than observer role
- `bookmark-track:` — most explicit, longest
- `probe:` — generic, loses the domain info

Happy to rename to any of those or something you like better. It's a prefix string sprinkled through `main.rs` + `notes/chores-05.md`; replace-all is a two-line edit. Want to swap it?

message.content: We use TProbe (Time Probe) for our actor-x1 project so it's not a great name.

content.text:
Fair point. Let me offer a few alternatives, aiming for "short, domain-specific, unambiguous":

- `tracking:` — simplest, says what it's about. Slight risk of colliding with the English word in some unrelated log line, but I don't see any in vc-x1's output today.
- `track-check:` — adds the verb. Clearer that it's a check, not a general status.
- `track-status:` — descriptive, slightly longer.
- `bm-track:` — explicitly names the bookmark concept. Short.

My lean: **`track-check:`** — one more character than `track-probe:` but reads as what it does ("we're checking tracking state"), doesn't collide with TProbe or the English word, and stays greppable.

Since 0.37.4 is still pre-commit and unrelated to the rename, two options:

1. **Fold rename into 0.37.4** — grep + replace-all `track-probe` → `track-check` across `src/main.rs` and `notes/chores-05.md`. One-line additional scope, same commit.
2. **Rename in a follow-up 0.37.5** — keeps 0.37.4 scope pure.

Your pick on the name AND the folding decision.

message.content: I vote bm-track

content.text: Going with `bm-track`, folded into 0.37.4 (small edit, still pre-commit). Flag me if you'd rather split.

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress


## Todo

A markdown list of task to do in the near feature

 - sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary (per-repo
   pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only fix shipped in
   0.37.1; this is the design+impl of the actual signal. [54]
 - Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both repos in
   one shot. Natural home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
 - Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards through to
   the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation). 0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`;
   `--no-check` would be the user-opt-in to "auto-rebase under the gates,
   I trust the sync state". Default stays `--check`.
 - track-probe "silent when clean" refinement. Always probe on entry
   and exit, but only *print* when the state isn't fully tracked or
   the exit state differs from entry. **When we do print on exit,
   include the entry state in the output** so the transition is
   explicit rather than inferred — "entry was ok (not printed), exit
   failed" is inference; "entry=tracked, exit=NOT_TRACKED" in one
   printed line is evidence. Record entry state in a local variable
   at the enter call site and pass into the exit call. Output shape:
   `track-probe vc-x1 <cmd>: enter=<state> → exit=<state>` — one
   line only when there's something to say. Preserves detection
   value, removes the steady-state two-lines-per-command noise, and
   keeps provenance unambiguous. Deferred in 0.37.4 until
   signal-confidence is established through more varied dogfood —
   changing output behavior before then would muddy the "is the
   probe itself reliable?" question.
 - `vc-x1 push`: state-sanity preflight on resume. Before any stage runs,
   verify saved state matches reality: `state.app_chid` still exists
   (not abandoned/rewritten)?  `main` bookmark at `state.app_chid`'s
   commit? `state.claude_chid` consistent with `.claude` working copy?
   On mismatch, refuse with a loud "state is stale — run `vc-x1 push
   --restart`" error. Surfaced 2026-04-22: 0.37.1 push errored at
   finalize-claude; out-of-band recovery (manual finalize + force-push
   of a squash) moved the world forward; 0.37.2 push then resumed at
   the parked finalize-claude, no-op'd, and falsely declared "completed
   all stages" while working copies still held uncommitted changes.
 - `vc-x1 push`: stage-prereq verification + honest completion. Each
   stage declares what it expects (working-copy dirty for commit-app;
   bookmark at specific commit for bookmark-both; etc.); dispatcher
   checks before running. "Completed all stages" should only print
   when stages genuinely ran or were verified-already-done, not when
   they were skipped without verification. Same dogfood surfaced this.
 - CLAUDE.md "Manual finalize fallback": document that push's state
   file must be cleared (`vc-x1 push --restart` or `rm`) after any
   out-of-band recovery (manual `vc-x1 finalize`, `jj squash
   --ignore-immutable` + force-push, etc.) so a later `vc-x1 push`
   doesn't resume from a now-stale halt point.
 - `vc-x1 test-fixture` should refuse `--path` values that resolve inside
   the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood surfaced this:
   `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj snapshot the fixture's bare-git
   remotes into the commit — a 56-file noise blob that got force-push'd
   off the remote later. Tool-level prevention, not `.gitignore`.
 - Tighten CLAUDE.md "Late changes after push" recipe: drop the
   `jj bookmark set <bookmark> -r @- -R .` line when the squash target
   is `@-` (bookmark follows naturally). Keep it only for the cases
   where squash lands somewhere the bookmark isn't already.
 - Non-tracking-remote bookmark detection across every repo-modifying
   command. Diagnosed 2026-04-22 dogfood: jj's tracking state is
   **per-workspace** (local `.jj` store), not shared via git refs.
   Sync across machines transfers refs but not the tracking flag —
   so a fresh workspace fetched-into never auto-tracks. The failure
   surfaces only when `jj git push` is attempted, which is too late
   (push-app already succeeded in our case).

   **Policy (decided):** error loudly, with the exact
   `jj bookmark track <b> --remote=<r> -R <repo>` remediation
   command. No self-heal — keeps the fix explicit and visible.

   **Scope (every command that creates or mutates repo state):**
   - `vc-x1 init`: already correct (Step 10 calls `jj bookmark track`).
     Add the check as a post-condition sanity assertion anyway.
   - `vc-x1 clone`: does `git clone` then `jj git init --colocate`.
     Whether that combination auto-establishes tracking in jj's
     workspace store is unclear; probably needs an explicit
     `jj bookmark track` for each cloned bookmark after the init.
     Verify and fix.
   - `vc-x1 test-fixture`: Step 7's `jj git push --bookmark main`
     establishes tracking as a side effect of the first push. Works
     correctly — confirmed via `jj bookmark list --tracked` on a
     fresh fixture. No change needed; the post-condition sanity
     check would naturally cover it.
   - `vc-x1 sync` preflight: detect + error.
   - `vc-x1 push` preflight: detect + error (before any mutation).
   - `vc-x1 finalize`: detect + error before the squash, so a
     failed push doesn't leave a half-finalized state.

   Shared helper: `common::verify_tracking(repo, bookmark, remote)
   -> Result<(), Err>` or similar. Probably use
   `jj bookmark list --tracked -T <template>` under the hood
   rather than parsing human-readable output.
 - Allow `vc-x1 push` to work on code or bot repo together or independantly and
   specifically we should be able to "squash" in th code repo just as we do in
   the bot repo.
 - Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
 - Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
 - Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
 - Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
 - Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
 - Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
 - Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
 - Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
 - Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]
- push polish: --dry-run, --step, non-tty detection, gitignore warning (0.37.0-5) [48]
- push docs + workflow migration — CLAUDE.md rewrite + README section (0.37.0) [48]
- First-dogfood polish for push: editor template, gitignore-fatal, sync --check, log prefix, quieter subprocess (0.37.1) [53]
- Temporary bookmark-tracking diagnostic probe on command entry/exit (0.37.2) [55]
- Fix two probe bugs: cwd-relative paths, strict @origin: parse (0.37.3) [56]
- Promote track-probe from temporary to permanent (0.37.4) [57]

## Promote bm-track from temporary to permanent (0.37.4)

User data point after the 0.37.3 bug fixes: "I've had it happen
more than once." That reframes the probe from "one-shot
investigation" (add, catch, rip out) to "permanent sanity check"
(always on, signals a real-but-rare class of failures).

Amendment to the 0.37.2 + 0.37.3 framing, not code behavior —
the probe itself is unchanged, only its docs and status.

- `src/main.rs` — `bm_track` doc comment rewritten. Drops
  "TEMPORARY" and the "Rip out once the culprit is identified"
  line. Adds context on why it stayed in place (multiple past
  occurrences reported) and notes the deferred "silent when
  clean" refinement that would preserve detection value while
  removing steady-state noise.
- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — `0.37.3` → `0.37.4`.
- `notes/todo.md` — Done line + new Todo for the quieter-mode
  refinement (silent when clean, loud on mismatch).

The ripout checklist in 0.37.2's subsection and the references
to "temporary" in 0.37.3's subsection are preserved as historical
record rather than edited in place — they accurately describe
the authors' intent *at the time those versions shipped*, and
rewriting them would erase the story of how the decision
evolved. Future readers find the authoritative status here
(subsection 0.37.4) via the normal chronological read.

## Promote bm-track from temporary to permanent + rename (0.37.4)

User data point after the 0.37.3 bug fixes: "I've had it happen
more than once." That reframes the probe from "one-shot
investigation" (add, catch, rip out) to "permanent sanity check"
(always on, signals a real-but-rare class of failures).

Also picked a less-collision-prone log prefix: original
`track-probe` overlaps with the actor-x1 sibling project's
"TProbe" (time probe) naming. Renamed to `bm-track` (short for
"bookmark tracking") — shorter, domain-specific, no collision.

Amendment to the 0.37.2 + 0.37.3 framing, not behavioral —
the probe's logic is unchanged, only its docs, status, and
identifier.

- `src/main.rs` — `bm_track` doc comment rewritten. Drops
  "TEMPORARY" and the "Rip out once the culprit is identified"
  line. Adds context on why it stayed in place (multiple past
  occurrences reported) and notes the deferred "silent when
  clean" refinement that would preserve detection value while
  removing steady-state noise.
- `src/main.rs` — functions and log prefix renamed:
  `track_probe` → `bm_track`, `track_probe_one` → `bm_track_one`,
  log prefix `track-probe` → `bm-track`. Greppable, consistent.
- `notes/chores-05.md` — earlier 0.37.2 + 0.37.3 subsections
  updated in place for the rename (they reference code symbols;
  those symbols have new names as of this commit). The
  "temporary" narrative framing in those subsections is
  preserved — that's the story of how intent evolved and
  rewriting it would erase the reasoning trail.
- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — `0.37.3` → `0.37.4`.
- `notes/todo.md` — Done line + new Todo for the quieter-mode
  refinement (silent when clean, loud on mismatch; entry state
  carried into the exit-line printing so the transition is
  evidence, not inference).

content.text:
Smoke test looks right — `bm-track` prefix, both tracked.

**On the interruption question**: Claude Code's built-in way to interrupt me mid-tool-call is **Esc** (once cancels the current tool call and gives you the prompt; the typed message gets read as my next turn). Anything you type while I'm working queues up and gets delivered when the current turn finishes — that's how your `/btw` pattern works.

There's no built-in `/pause` or `/stop` slash command. Two options if you want explicit ones:

1. **Just use Esc as "/stop"** — it's the terminal-native interrupt. One key, always available, cancels cleanly.
2. **Define a custom slash skill** — a `/pause` skill in `~/.claude/skills/pause/SKILL.md` that just says "stop and wait for the user" would give you a typed command that does the same thing. Happy to help you set that up separately.

For quick "hold on" moments during a long task, Esc is the fastest path. For "stop this specific line of thought and talk first", queuing a text message (no slash needed — just type) is usually enough since I read it at the next safe point.

Now back to the commit. Same proposed title/body with a small addition to reflect the rename:

**Title** (42 chars):

```
docs: promote bm-track to permanent (0.37.4)
```

**Body**:

```
User data point after 0.37.3: "I've had it happen more than
once." Reframes the probe from one-shot investigation to
permanent sanity check. Also renames log prefix and functions
from `track-probe` / `track_probe*` to `bm-track` / `bm_track*`
to avoid collision with actor-x1's TProbe (time probe).

- src/main.rs: track_probe → bm_track, track_probe_one →
  bm_track_one, log prefix `track-probe` → `bm-track`. Doc
  comment drops "TEMPORARY" and the ripout language; adds
  context on why it's permanent and the deferred "silent
  when clean" refinement
- Cargo.toml / Cargo.lock: 0.37.3 → 0.37.4
- notes/chores-05.md: new 0.37.4 subsection; earlier 0.37.2 +
  0.37.3 subsections updated in place for the rename (their
  "temporary" narrative framing is preserved)
- notes/todo.md: Done line + new Todo for the silent-when-clean
  refinement (including: print entry state in the exit line
  when firing, so the transition is evidence not inference)
```

OK to run?

765	## Temporary bookmark-tracking diagnostic probe (0.37.2)
766	
767	0.37.1's first-dogfood push hit a `bookmark 'main' has non-tracking
768	remote 'main@origin'` error in the `.claude` repo during the
769	`finalize-claude` stage. Recovery was one-time: `jj bookmark track
770	main --remote=origin -R .claude`. But the open question is *when*
771	and *why* the tracking flag got lost — the fwlaptop op log shows
772	`.claude` pushing fine from there, so the 3900x workspace ended
773	up in a different state at some historical point.
774	
775	The detect-and-error work (`notes/todo.md` top entry) is the
776	long-term policy fix. This chore is a **temporary diagnostic**
777	to localize the culprit: probe `main`-bookmark tracking status on
778	entry and exit of every vc-x1 command. If entry and exit differ
779	during a single command, that command is the culprit; if entry
780	differs from the previous command's exit, something *between*
781	invocations broke it (external tooling, manual jj commands, etc.).
782	
783	Output shape:
784	
785	```
786	bm-track enter vc-x1 push: app(main)=tracked, .claude(main)=tracked
787	bm-track exit  vc-x1 push: app(main)=tracked, .claude(main)=NOT_TRACKED
788	```
789	
790	- `src/main.rs` — `use std::path::Path;` added. Two new functions:
791	  `bm_track(phase, command_name)` prints the status line for
792	  both repos; `bm_track_one(repo, bookmark, remote)` queries
793	  jj via `jj bookmark list --tracked <bookmark> -R <repo>`. The
794	  tracked-list entry includes a line starting with `@<remote>:`
795	  when the bookmark is tracking that remote; absence means
796	  not-tracking.
797	- `src/main.rs` — dispatch block wraps the `match cli.command`
798	  in a `let exit_code = match ...;` binding, with `bm_track`
799	  calls immediately before and after. Skipped when `is_detached_exec`
800	  (the `finalize --exec` re-entry) since probe noise in that log
801	  isn't useful.
802	- Graceful degradation — if the repo doesn't exist, missing-`.jj/`
803	  detection emits `no-jj`; subprocess failure emits `err(<first line>)`.
804	  Never blocks the wrapped command.
805	- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — `0.37.1` → `0.37.2`.
806	
807	**How to rip it out** once the culprit is identified:
808	
809	```bash
810	grep -rn 'bm-track\|bm_track' src/
811	```
812	
813	Three places to touch:
814	1. `src/main.rs` — delete the two functions (`bm_track`,
815	   `bm_track_one`) and the `use std::path::Path` import if
816	   nothing else needs it.
817	2. `src/main.rs` — revert the dispatch to plain
818	   `match cli.command { ... }` (drop the `let exit_code = ...`
819	   binding, the two `bm_track(...)` calls, and the
820	   `command_name` binding).
821	3. `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — bump to the next appropriate
822	   version.
823	
824	Net removal: ~80 lines.
825	
826	## Fix two probe bugs (0.37.3)
827	
828	First use of the 0.37.2 probe caught two bugs — in the probe itself,
829	not in vc-x1's push flow.

file.content:
## Temporary bookmark-tracking diagnostic probe (0.37.2)

0.37.1's first-dogfood push hit a `bookmark 'main' has non-tracking
remote 'main@origin'` error in the `.claude` repo during the
`finalize-claude` stage. Recovery was one-time: `jj bookmark track
main --remote=origin -R .claude`. But the open question is *when*
and *why* the tracking flag got lost — the fwlaptop op log shows
`.claude` pushing fine from there, so the 3900x workspace ended
up in a different state at some historical point.

The detect-and-error work (`notes/todo.md` top entry) is the
long-term policy fix. This chore is a **temporary diagnostic**
to localize the culprit: probe `main`-bookmark tracking status on
entry and exit of every vc-x1 command. If entry and exit differ
during a single command, that command is the culprit; if entry
differs from the previous command's exit, something *between*
invocations broke it (external tooling, manual jj commands, etc.).

Output shape:

```
bm-track enter vc-x1 push: app(main)=tracked, .claude(main)=tracked
bm-track exit  vc-x1 push: app(main)=tracked, .claude(main)=NOT_TRACKED
```

- `src/main.rs` — `use std::path::Path;` added. Two new functions:
  `bm_track(phase, command_name)` prints the status line for
  both repos; `bm_track_one(repo, bookmark, remote)` queries
  jj via `jj bookmark list --tracked <bookmark> -R <repo>`. The
  tracked-list entry includes a line starting with `@<remote>:`
  when the bookmark is tracking that remote; absence means
  not-tracking.
- `src/main.rs` — dispatch block wraps the `match cli.command`
  in a `let exit_code = match ...;` binding, with `bm_track`
  calls immediately before and after. Skipped when `is_detached_exec`
  (the `finalize --exec` re-entry) since probe noise in that log
  isn't useful.
- Graceful degradation — if the repo doesn't exist, missing-`.jj/`
  detection emits `no-jj`; subprocess failure emits `err(<first line>)`.
  Never blocks the wrapped command.
- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — `0.37.1` → `0.37.2`.

**How to rip it out** once the culprit is identified:

```bash
grep -rn 'bm-track\|bm_track' src/
```

Three places to touch:
1. `src/main.rs` — delete the two functions (`bm_track`,
   `bm_track_one`) and the `use std::path::Path` import if
   nothing else needs it.
2. `src/main.rs` — revert the dispatch to plain
   `match cli.command { ... }` (drop the `let exit_code = ...`
   binding, the two `bm_track(...)` calls, and the
   `command_name` binding).
3. `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — bump to the next appropriate
   version.

Net removal: ~80 lines.

## Fix two probe bugs (0.37.3)

First use of the 0.37.2 probe caught two bugs — in the probe itself,
not in vc-x1's push flow.

input.new_string:
Output shape (as originally shipped in 0.37.2; renamed to
`bm-track` in 0.37.3):

```
track-probe enter vc-x1 push: app(main)=tracked, .claude(main)=tracked
track-probe exit  vc-x1 push: app(main)=tracked, .claude(main)=NOT_TRACKED
```

- `src/main.rs` — `use std::path::Path;` added. Two new functions:
  `track_probe(phase, command_name)` prints the status line for
  both repos; `track_probe_one(repo, bookmark, remote)` queries
  jj via `jj bookmark list --tracked <bookmark> -R <repo>`. The
  tracked-list entry includes a line starting with `@<remote>:`
  when the bookmark is tracking that remote; absence means
  not-tracking.
- `src/main.rs` — dispatch block wraps the `match cli.command`
  in a `let exit_code = match ...;` binding, with `track_probe`
  calls immediately before and after. Skipped when `is_detached_exec`
  (the `finalize --exec` re-entry) since probe noise in that log
  isn't useful.
- Graceful degradation — if the repo doesn't exist, missing-`.jj/`
  detection emits `no-jj`; subprocess failure emits `err(<first line>)`.
  Never blocks the wrapped command.
- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — `0.37.1` → `0.37.2`.

**How to rip it out** once the culprit is identified (as framed
at the time of 0.37.2; superseded in 0.37.3 when the probe was
promoted to permanent):

```bash

828	
829	## Fix two probe bugs (0.37.3)
830	
831	First use of the 0.37.2 probe caught two bugs — in the probe itself,
832	not in vc-x1's push flow.
833	
834	**Bug A — cwd-relative paths mislabel and miss.** The probe hardcoded
835	`Path::new(".")` and `Path::new(".claude")` as the repo paths. Run
836	from inside `.claude`, those resolve to `.claude` and `.claude/.claude`
837	respectively — probe reported `app(main)=tracked, .claude(main)=no-jj`,
838	which is doubly wrong: the "app" column is actually showing `.claude`'s
839	status, and the ".claude" column is showing a nonexistent path. Fix:
840	walk up from cwd looking for `.vc-config.toml` with `path = "/"`.
841	That file marks the app-repo side of the dual-repo pair, so its
842	containing dir is the workspace root. Probe `<root>` and
843	`<root>/.claude` unconditionally — gives consistent labeling from
844	any cwd inside the workspace. Outside any workspace, probe prints
845	`no-workspace` and skips.
846	
847	**Bug B — `@origin:` parse misses the divergent-decorated form.**
848	Probe checked `line.trim_start().starts_with("@origin:")`. But when
849	local `main` is ahead of `@origin`, jj decorates the line as
850	`@origin (ahead by 1 commits): <old_commit>` — same tracking
851	relationship, different prefix, my check fails. **This is what
852	produced the scary-looking `NOT_TRACKED` at 0.37.2's push-exit
853	probe** — the bookmark WAS tracking, it was just briefly
854	local-ahead between `bookmark-both` and the detached finalize's
855	push. False positive. Fix: accept both `@origin:` (synced) and
856	`@origin ` (divergent-decorated, note trailing space) as tracking
857	markers. The tracking relationship is what matters; sync-state is
858	orthogonal.
859	
860	- `src/main.rs` — `bm_track` rewritten to call
861	  `find_workspace_root()` first, then probe `<root>` + `<root>/.claude`.
862	  Graceful fallback to `no-workspace` when cwd is outside any vc-x1
863	  workspace.
864	- `src/main.rs` — new `find_workspace_root()` helper. Walks up from
865	  cwd reading each `.vc-config.toml` it finds; matches if the file
866	  has `path = "/"` (with or without surrounding whitespace). Returns
867	  `None` at filesystem root if no match found.
868	- `src/main.rs` — `bm_track_one` tracking detection loosened.
869	  Now accepts either `@{remote}:` or `@{remote} ` (trailing space
870	  for the decorated form). Doc comment expanded to call out both
871	  cases.
872	- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — `0.37.2` → `0.37.3`.
873	
874	Implication for the chain of diagnoses: the `NOT_TRACKED` seen at
875	0.37.2's push-exit probe was a false positive. There is no evidence
876	yet of an actual tracking-loss bug inside vc-x1's push flow. The
877	*original* tracking loss that triggered the 0.37.1 finalize-claude
878	failure was real (`jj git push` genuinely refused), but that loss
879	happened before any probe existed — origin lies somewhere in
880	`.claude`'s historical bootstrap, not in the recent commit+push
881	sequence. The probe continues to provide value going forward: it
882	would catch any new tracking-loss the moment it happens.
883	
884	## Promote bm-track from temporary to permanent + rename (0.37.4)
885	
886	User data point after the 0.37.3 bug fixes: "I've had it happen
887	more than once." That reframes the probe from "one-shot
888	investigation" (add, catch, rip out) to "permanent sanity check"
889	(always on, signals a real-but-rare class of failures).
890	
891	Also picked a less-collision-prone log prefix: original
892	`track-probe` overlaps with the actor-x1 sibling project's
893	"TProbe" (time probe) naming. Renamed to `bm-track` (short for
894	"bookmark tracking") — shorter, domain-specific, no collision.
895	
896	Amendment to the 0.37.2 + 0.37.3 framing, not behavioral —
897	the probe's logic is unchanged, only its docs, status, and
898	identifier.
899	
900	- `src/main.rs` — `bm_track` doc comment rewritten. Drops
901	  "TEMPORARY" and the "Rip out once the culprit is identified"
902	  line. Adds context on why it stayed in place (multiple past
903	  occurrences reported) and notes the deferred "silent when
904	  clean" refinement that would preserve detection value while
905	  removing steady-state noise.
906	- `src/main.rs` — functions and log prefix renamed:
907	  `track_probe` → `bm_track`, `track_probe_one` → `bm_track_one`,
908	  log prefix `track-probe` → `bm-track`. Greppable, consistent.
909	- `notes/chores-05.md` — earlier 0.37.2 + 0.37.3 subsections
910	  updated in place for the rename (they reference code symbols;
911	  those symbols have new names as of this commit). The
912	  "temporary" narrative framing in those subsections is
913	  preserved — that's the story of how intent evolved and
914	  rewriting it would erase the reasoning trail.
915	- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — `0.37.3` → `0.37.4`.

file.content:
## Fix two probe bugs (0.37.3)

First use of the 0.37.2 probe caught two bugs — in the probe itself,
not in vc-x1's push flow.

**Bug A — cwd-relative paths mislabel and miss.** The probe hardcoded
`Path::new(".")` and `Path::new(".claude")` as the repo paths. Run
from inside `.claude`, those resolve to `.claude` and `.claude/.claude`
respectively — probe reported `app(main)=tracked, .claude(main)=no-jj`,
which is doubly wrong: the "app" column is actually showing `.claude`'s
status, and the ".claude" column is showing a nonexistent path. Fix:
walk up from cwd looking for `.vc-config.toml` with `path = "/"`.
That file marks the app-repo side of the dual-repo pair, so its
containing dir is the workspace root. Probe `<root>` and
`<root>/.claude` unconditionally — gives consistent labeling from
any cwd inside the workspace. Outside any workspace, probe prints
`no-workspace` and skips.

**Bug B — `@origin:` parse misses the divergent-decorated form.**
Probe checked `line.trim_start().starts_with("@origin:")`. But when
local `main` is ahead of `@origin`, jj decorates the line as
`@origin (ahead by 1 commits): <old_commit>` — same tracking
relationship, different prefix, my check fails. **This is what
produced the scary-looking `NOT_TRACKED` at 0.37.2's push-exit
probe** — the bookmark WAS tracking, it was just briefly
local-ahead between `bookmark-both` and the detached finalize's
push. False positive. Fix: accept both `@origin:` (synced) and
`@origin ` (divergent-decorated, note trailing space) as tracking
markers. The tracking relationship is what matters; sync-state is
orthogonal.

- `src/main.rs` — `bm_track` rewritten to call
  `find_workspace_root()` first, then probe `<root>` + `<root>/.claude`.
  Graceful fallback to `no-workspace` when cwd is outside any vc-x1
  workspace.
- `src/main.rs` — new `find_workspace_root()` helper. Walks up from
  cwd reading each `.vc-config.toml` it finds; matches if the file
  has `path = "/"` (with or without surrounding whitespace). Returns
  `None` at filesystem root if no match found.
- `src/main.rs` — `bm_track_one` tracking detection loosened.
  Now accepts either `@{remote}:` or `@{remote} ` (trailing space
  for the decorated form). Doc comment expanded to call out both
  cases.
- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — `0.37.2` → `0.37.3`.

Implication for the chain of diagnoses: the `NOT_TRACKED` seen at
0.37.2's push-exit probe was a false positive. There is no evidence
yet of an actual tracking-loss bug inside vc-x1's push flow. The
*original* tracking loss that triggered the 0.37.1 finalize-claude
failure was real (`jj git push` genuinely refused), but that loss
happened before any probe existed — origin lies somewhere in
`.claude`'s historical bootstrap, not in the recent commit+push
sequence. The probe continues to provide value going forward: it
would catch any new tracking-loss the moment it happens.

## Promote bm-track from temporary to permanent + rename (0.37.4)

User data point after the 0.37.3 bug fixes: "I've had it happen
more than once." That reframes the probe from "one-shot
investigation" (add, catch, rip out) to "permanent sanity check"
(always on, signals a real-but-rare class of failures).

Also picked a less-collision-prone log prefix: original
`track-probe` overlaps with the actor-x1 sibling project's
"TProbe" (time probe) naming. Renamed to `bm-track` (short for
"bookmark tracking") — shorter, domain-specific, no collision.

Amendment to the 0.37.2 + 0.37.3 framing, not behavioral —
the probe's logic is unchanged, only its docs, status, and
identifier.

- `src/main.rs` — `bm_track` doc comment rewritten. Drops
  "TEMPORARY" and the "Rip out once the culprit is identified"
  line. Adds context on why it stayed in place (multiple past
  occurrences reported) and notes the deferred "silent when
  clean" refinement that would preserve detection value while
  removing steady-state noise.
- `src/main.rs` — functions and log prefix renamed:
  `track_probe` → `bm_track`, `track_probe_one` → `bm_track_one`,
  log prefix `track-probe` → `bm-track`. Greppable, consistent.
- `notes/chores-05.md` — earlier 0.37.2 + 0.37.3 subsections
  updated in place for the rename (they reference code symbols;
  those symbols have new names as of this commit). The
  "temporary" narrative framing in those subsections is
  preserved — that's the story of how intent evolved and
  rewriting it would erase the reasoning trail.
- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — `0.37.3` → `0.37.4`.
- `notes/todo.md` — Done line + new Todo for the quieter-mode
  refinement (silent when clean, loud on mismatch; entry state
  carried into the exit-line printing so the transition is
  evidence, not inference).

input.new_string:
## Fix bm-track bugs + rename + promote to permanent (0.37.3)

Three concerns bundled into this patch — all tied to the
bookmark-tracking probe added in 0.37.2:

1. **Two bugs in the probe itself** (the original motivation for
   0.37.3). First real use in 0.37.2 caught them — not vc-x1's
   push flow, as the output had misleadingly suggested.
2. **Rename** from `track-probe` / `track_probe*` to `bm-track` /
   `bm_track*` to avoid collision with the actor-x1 sibling
   project's "TProbe" (time probe) naming.
3. **Promote from temporary to permanent.** User data point:
   "I've had it happen more than once." Drops the 0.37.2
   ripout-checklist framing in favor of a stays-in-place sanity
   check.

### Bug A — cwd-relative paths mislabel and miss

The 0.37.2 probe hardcoded `Path::new(".")` and
`Path::new(".claude")` as the repo paths. Run from inside
`.claude`, those resolve to `.claude` and `.claude/.claude`
respectively — probe reported `app(main)=tracked,
.claude(main)=no-jj`, which is doubly wrong: the "app" column
is actually showing `.claude`'s status, and the ".claude" column
is showing a nonexistent path. Fix: walk up from cwd looking for
`.vc-config.toml` with `path = "/"`. That file marks the app-repo
side of the dual-repo pair, so its containing dir is the
workspace root. Probe `<root>` and `<root>/.claude` unconditionally
— gives consistent labeling from any cwd inside the workspace.
Outside any workspace, probe prints `no-workspace` and skips.

### Bug B — `@origin:` parse misses the divergent-decorated form

The 0.37.2 probe checked `line.trim_start().starts_with("@origin:")`.
But when local `main` is ahead of `@origin`, jj decorates the line
as `@origin (ahead by 1 commits): <old_commit>` — same tracking
relationship, different prefix, the check fails. **This is what
produced the scary-looking `NOT_TRACKED` at 0.37.2's push-exit
probe** — the bookmark WAS tracking, it was just briefly
local-ahead between `bookmark-both` and the detached finalize's
push. False positive. Fix: accept both `@origin:` (synced) and
`@origin ` (divergent-decorated, trailing space) as tracking
markers. The tracking relationship is what matters; sync-state is
orthogonal.

### Rename

Log prefix `track-probe` in 0.37.2's output collides conceptually
with actor-x1's "TProbe" (time probe). Renamed here to `bm-track`
(short for "bookmark tracking") — shorter, domain-specific, no
collision. Grep-replaced across source and the current chores
entry; the 0.37.2 subsection above was left using the original
`track-probe` name for historical accuracy at that shipping point.

### Promote to permanent

0.37.2 framed the probe as temporary with a ripout checklist.
User confirmed "happens more than once" — i.e., the tracking-loss
class of failures is rare but real, and a permanent always-on
sanity check beats waiting to rip the probe out after one catch.
Probe logic unchanged; only its doc comment and the 0.37.2
ripout-checklist framing are retired (the checklist is left in
the 0.37.2 subsection as historical record, annotated as
superseded here).

### Edits

- `src/main.rs` — `bm_track` rewritten to call
  `find_workspace_root()` first, then probe `<root>` +
  `<root>/.claude`. Graceful fallback to `no-workspace` when cwd
  is outside any vc-x1 workspace.
- `src/main.rs` — new `find_workspace_root()` helper. Walks up
  from cwd reading each `.vc-config.toml` it finds; matches if
  the file has `path = "/"` (with or without surrounding
  whitespace). Returns `None` at filesystem root if no match
  found.
- `src/main.rs` — `bm_track_one` tracking detection loosened.
  Now accepts either `@{remote}:` or `@{remote} ` (trailing
  space for the decorated form). Doc comment expanded to call
  out both cases.
- `src/main.rs` — functions and log prefix renamed:
  `track_probe` → `bm_track`, `track_probe_one` →
  `bm_track_one`, log prefix `track-probe` → `bm-track`.
- `src/main.rs` — `bm_track` doc comment drops "TEMPORARY" and
  the "Rip out once the culprit is identified" line. Adds context
  on why it stays in place (multiple past occurrences reported)
  and notes the deferred "silent when clean" refinement that
  would preserve detection value while removing steady-state
  noise (see `notes/todo.md`).
- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — `0.37.2` → `0.37.3`.

Implication for the chain of diagnoses: the `NOT_TRACKED` seen
at 0.37.2's push-exit probe was a false positive from Bug B.
There is no evidence yet of an actual tracking-loss bug inside
vc-x1's push flow. The *original* tracking loss that triggered
the 0.37.1 finalize-claude failure was real (`jj git push`
genuinely refused), but that loss happened before any probe
existed — origin lies somewhere in `.claude`'s historical
bootstrap, not in the recent commit+push sequence. The probe
(now permanent) continues to provide value going forward: it
would catch any new tracking-loss the moment it happens.

## Fix two probe bugs (0.37.3)

First use of the 0.37.2 probe caught two bugs — in the probe itself,
not in vc-x1's push flow.

**Bug A — cwd-relative paths mislabel and miss.** The probe hardcoded
`Path::new(".")` and `Path::new(".claude")` as the repo paths. Run
from inside `.claude`, those resolve to `.claude` and `.claude/.claude`
respectively — probe reported `app(main)=tracked, .claude(main)=no-jj`,
which is doubly wrong: the "app" column is actually showing `.claude`'s
status, and the ".claude" column is showing a nonexistent path. Fix:
walk up from cwd looking for `.vc-config.toml` with `path = "/"`.
That file marks the app-repo side of the dual-repo pair, so its
containing dir is the workspace root. Probe `<root>` and
`<root>/.claude` unconditionally — gives consistent labeling from
any cwd inside the workspace. Outside any workspace, probe prints
`no-workspace` and skips.

**Bug B — `@origin:` parse misses the divergent-decorated form.**
Probe checked `line.trim_start().starts_with("@origin:")`. But when
local `main` is ahead of `@origin`, jj decorates the line as
`@origin (ahead by 1 commits): <old_commit>` — same tracking
relationship, different prefix, my check fails. **This is what
produced the scary-looking `NOT_TRACKED` at 0.37.2's push-exit
probe** — the bookmark WAS tracking, it was just briefly
local-ahead between `bookmark-both` and the detached finalize's
push. False positive. Fix: accept both `@origin:` (synced) and
`@origin ` (divergent-decorated, note trailing space) as tracking
markers. The tracking relationship is what matters; sync-state is
orthogonal.

- `src/main.rs` — `bm_track` rewritten to call
  `find_workspace_root()` first, then probe `<root>` + `<root>/.claude`.
  Graceful fallback to `no-workspace` when cwd is outside any vc-x1
  workspace.
- `src/main.rs` — new `find_workspace_root()` helper. Walks up from
  cwd reading each `.vc-config.toml` it finds; matches if the file
  has `path = "/"` (with or without surrounding whitespace). Returns
  `None` at filesystem root if no match found.
- `src/main.rs` — `bm_track_one` tracking detection loosened.
  Now accepts either `@{remote}:` or `@{remote} ` (trailing space
  for the decorated form). Doc comment expanded to call out both
  cases.
- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — `0.37.2` → `0.37.3`.

Implication for the chain of diagnoses: the `NOT_TRACKED` seen at
0.37.2's push-exit probe was a false positive. There is no evidence
yet of an actual tracking-loss bug inside vc-x1's push flow. The
*original* tracking loss that triggered the 0.37.1 finalize-claude
failure was real (`jj git push` genuinely refused), but that loss
happened before any probe existed — origin lies somewhere in
`.claude`'s historical bootstrap, not in the recent commit+push
sequence. The probe continues to provide value going forward: it
would catch any new tracking-loss the moment it happens.

## Promote bm-track from temporary to permanent + rename (0.37.4)

User data point after the 0.37.3 bug fixes: "I've had it happen
more than once." That reframes the probe from "one-shot
investigation" (add, catch, rip out) to "permanent sanity check"
(always on, signals a real-but-rare class of failures).

Also picked a less-collision-prone log prefix: original
`track-probe` overlaps with the actor-x1 sibling project's
"TProbe" (time probe) naming. Renamed to `bm-track` (short for
"bookmark tracking") — shorter, domain-specific, no collision.

Amendment to the 0.37.2 + 0.37.3 framing, not behavioral —
the probe's logic is unchanged, only its docs, status, and
identifier.

- `src/main.rs` — `bm_track` doc comment rewritten. Drops
  "TEMPORARY" and the "Rip out once the culprit is identified"
  line. Adds context on why it stayed in place (multiple past
  occurrences reported) and notes the deferred "silent when
  clean" refinement that would preserve detection value while
  removing steady-state noise.
- `src/main.rs` — functions and log prefix renamed:
  `track_probe` → `bm_track`, `track_probe_one` → `bm_track_one`,
  log prefix `track-probe` → `bm-track`. Greppable, consistent.
- `notes/chores-05.md` — earlier 0.37.2 + 0.37.3 subsections
  updated in place for the rename (they reference code symbols;
  those symbols have new names as of this commit). The
  "temporary" narrative framing in those subsections is
  preserved — that's the story of how intent evolved and
  rewriting it would erase the reasoning trail.
- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — `0.37.3` → `0.37.4`.
- `notes/todo.md` — Done line + new Todo for the quieter-mode
  refinement (silent when clean, loud on mismatch; entry state
  carried into the exit-line printing so the transition is
  evidence, not inference).

## Fix bm-track bugs + rename + promote to permanent (0.37.3)

Three concerns bundled into this patch — all tied to the
bookmark-tracking probe added in 0.37.2:

1. **Two bugs in the probe itself** (the original motivation for
   0.37.3). First real use in 0.37.2 caught them — not vc-x1's
   push flow, as the output had misleadingly suggested.
2. **Rename** from `track-probe` / `track_probe*` to `bm-track` /
   `bm_track*` to avoid collision with the actor-x1 sibling
   project's "TProbe" (time probe) naming.
3. **Promote from temporary to permanent.** User data point:
   "I've had it happen more than once." Drops the 0.37.2
   ripout-checklist framing in favor of a stays-in-place sanity
   check.

### Bug A — cwd-relative paths mislabel and miss

The 0.37.2 probe hardcoded `Path::new(".")` and
`Path::new(".claude")` as the repo paths. Run from inside
`.claude`, those resolve to `.claude` and `.claude/.claude`
respectively — probe reported `app(main)=tracked,
.claude(main)=no-jj`, which is doubly wrong: the "app" column
is actually showing `.claude`'s status, and the ".claude" column
is showing a nonexistent path. Fix: walk up from cwd looking for
`.vc-config.toml` with `path = "/"`. That file marks the app-repo
side of the dual-repo pair, so its containing dir is the
workspace root. Probe `<root>` and `<root>/.claude` unconditionally
— gives consistent labeling from any cwd inside the workspace.
Outside any workspace, probe prints `no-workspace` and skips.

### Bug B — `@origin:` parse misses the divergent-decorated form

The 0.37.2 probe checked `line.trim_start().starts_with("@origin:")`.
But when local `main` is ahead of `@origin`, jj decorates the line
as `@origin (ahead by 1 commits): <old_commit>` — same tracking
relationship, different prefix, the check fails. **This is what
produced the scary-looking `NOT_TRACKED` at 0.37.2's push-exit
probe** — the bookmark WAS tracking, it was just briefly
local-ahead between `bookmark-both` and the detached finalize's
push. False positive. Fix: accept both `@origin:` (synced) and
`@origin ` (divergent-decorated, trailing space) as tracking
markers. The tracking relationship is what matters; sync-state is
orthogonal.

### Rename

Log prefix `track-probe` in 0.37.2's output collides conceptually
with actor-x1's "TProbe" (time probe). Renamed here to `bm-track`
(short for "bookmark tracking") — shorter, domain-specific, no
collision. Grep-replaced across source and the current chores
entry; the 0.37.2 subsection above was left using the original
`track-probe` name for historical accuracy at that shipping point.

### Promote to permanent

0.37.2 framed the probe as temporary with a ripout checklist.
User confirmed "happens more than once" — i.e., the tracking-loss
class of failures is rare but real, and a permanent always-on
sanity check beats waiting to rip the probe out after one catch.
Probe logic unchanged; only its doc comment and the 0.37.2
ripout-checklist framing are retired (the checklist is left in
the 0.37.2 subsection as historical record, annotated as
superseded here).

### Edits

- `src/main.rs` — `bm_track` rewritten to call
  `find_workspace_root()` first, then probe `<root>` +
  `<root>/.claude`. Graceful fallback to `no-workspace` when cwd
  is outside any vc-x1 workspace.
- `src/main.rs` — new `find_workspace_root()` helper. Walks up
  from cwd reading each `.vc-config.toml` it finds; matches if
  the file has `path = "/"` (with or without surrounding
  whitespace). Returns `None` at filesystem root if no match
  found.
- `src/main.rs` — `bm_track_one` tracking detection loosened.
  Now accepts either `@{remote}:` or `@{remote} ` (trailing
  space for the decorated form). Doc comment expanded to call
  out both cases.
- `src/main.rs` — functions and log prefix renamed:
  `track_probe` → `bm_track`, `track_probe_one` →
  `bm_track_one`, log prefix `track-probe` → `bm-track`.
- `src/main.rs` — `bm_track` doc comment drops "TEMPORARY" and
  the "Rip out once the culprit is identified" line. Adds context
  on why it stays in place (multiple past occurrences reported)
  and notes the deferred "silent when clean" refinement that
  would preserve detection value while removing steady-state
  noise (see `notes/todo.md`).
- `Cargo.toml` / `Cargo.lock` — `0.37.2` → `0.37.3`.

Implication for the chain of diagnoses: the `NOT_TRACKED` seen
at 0.37.2's push-exit probe was a false positive from Bug B.
There is no evidence yet of an actual tracking-loss bug inside
vc-x1's push flow. The *original* tracking loss that triggered
the 0.37.1 finalize-claude failure was real (`jj git push`
genuinely refused), but that loss happened before any probe
existed — origin lies somewhere in `.claude`'s historical
bootstrap, not in the recent commit+push sequence. The probe
(now permanent) continues to provide value going forward: it
would catch any new tracking-loss the moment it happens.
